title: 0xVoid ✈️
text:
# 0xVoid ✈️

The sharpest degen-trader voice among this chat's launch-week regulars —
articulate, leverage-obsessed, and the one who put the community's
diamond-hands rhetoric into its clearest words. Like the other high-volume
launch-era figures on this wiki, 0xVoid's entire documented presence (1,737
messages) sits in a short, front-loaded window: [27 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md) to [6 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-06.md) — the narrowest span of the four
covered here, barely ten days, all in the first week and a half after
launch.

## Hedge-everything trading style

0xVoid's opening messages set the tone: playing all three Tate-adjacent
coins at once as an explicit hedge, not a conviction bet — *"Honestly I’m
playing all 3 in case 🤣"* — [27 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md). Recurring threads:

- Detailed position-tracking: entries, re-entries, leverage sizing, and P&L
  reported in SOL, e.g. *"Took a -12 sol loss on $TATE but am up like +115
  on $TateAI"* — [29 Oct
  2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md).
- Genuine on-chain investigation — dev wallet tracing, testing whether
  CEX-to-token transfers are traceable on Solscan — in the same register as
  [[hokus_pokus]].
- Informal moderation: asks for ban/mute perms multiple times, personally
  de-escalates conflicts ("Spyro is a cool dude he just tryna make everyone
  money while we wait. We can tell him to do it in a different chat not
  here."), and issues soft threats to spammers ("You have 1 min b4 ban if
  not bot 🤣").

## The diamond-hands manifesto

This is the source of a quote worth verifying carefully, since it's easy to
flatten into something more generic than what was actually said. On [29 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md), 0xVoid writes a multi-message
argument for why this community's version of diamond-handing is different
from the earlier `$RNT`/`$DADDY` cycles (see [[diamond-hands]]):

> "He wants people this time to put in the work because the communities who
> are strong are those who put in the WORK. Leads to a more cult vibe and
> more diamond hands than people just riding off Andrew's pump, and jeeting
> and leaving."

> "This time is different, I'm putting in the work, actually taking the time
> to understand wtf is going on, and I'm definitely going to be diamond
> handing this even more because of that and this community we have is too
> retarded. Yall retards got me going schizo spamming penis reduction, and
> therefore I am part of the cult and will diamond hand my bags."

The "diamond handing this even more because of the community" framing does
hold up as a real, sincere-sounding statement — it's just embedded in a much
longer, funnier, more self-aware riff than the phrase alone suggests.

## The "penis reduction" raid

On the morning of [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md),
0xVoid is a central instigator of one of the chat's most absurd coordinated
bits: dozens of members, 0xVoid heavily among them, spam the phrase "penis
reduction" for over an hour on the theory that the `@tate_terminal` bot (see
[[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]]) was reading the chat and could be baited
into tweeting it — complete with slash-command chants
(`/PENIS_REDUCTION_TATEAI`) and real-time theorizing about the bot's tweet
queue. It's presented as pure schizo-posting for its own sake, not a serious
theory: *"i am turning schizo... i have been schizo since day 1."*

A separate, calmer catchphrase — *"preparations have been met"* — recurs
through early November as a dry, deadpan response to good news, e.g. [2 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md).

## First message

> "I’m in both coins played TateAI first"
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md)

## Related
[[diamond-hands]], [[hokus_pokus]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]]

# 🄰🄽🄰🄳 🅂🅄🄹🄸

The display name is rendered entirely in stylized Unicode
enclosed-alphanumeric characters — a decorative font choice, not a parsing
artifact — and reads in plain text as roughly "Anad Suji." One of the
earliest voices in the whole archive: first message at 02:10 on
[26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md), the group's opening
day, predating even the chat's first posted contract-address thesis that
same morning. Despite the highest message count of the three entities
covered in this ingest pass, the entire presence is tightly front-loaded —
essentially all 1,022 messages land between
[26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md) and
[2 Feb 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-02/2025-02-02.md), a roughly three-month
window, with nothing after.

## Voice: broken-English shorthand and constant media spam

A large share of the message count is bare stickers and GIFs with no text —
single sessions of 20-30 consecutive reaction-only posts are common. When
there is text, it's in a consistent, distinctive shorthand style that sets
this account apart from the more standard-English voices of [[ronald]] or
[[et]]: "wen" (when), "gud" (good), "wat"/"wats" (what), "hav" (have),
"tink" (think), "evone" (everyone), "milly" (million), "pamp"/"pamping"
(pump/pumping), "riskfree" written as one word and used constantly as a
personal catchphrase for having already recovered his initial stake.

## Day one: the "shakeout" theory

Within minutes of the group's founding, before any confirmed ticker or CA
existed, this account is already running the chat's core interpretive
frame — that price drops are Andrew Tate deliberately shaking out weak
hands, not real risk:

> "blockchain baby / tate created this coin / just need to spread the word"
> ... "he doin shakeout / so he can laugh at brokies later"
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)

## Participating in the "BOB" CA reading

By [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md), the account is
repeating the by-then-circulating "BOB" Easter-egg reading of the contract
address's first three characters (see [[contract-address]] for its
original attribution to "P" on 27 Oct):

> "wats the ca / 'BOB'" ... "ca is BOB"
> — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)

The following day he also produces an independent piece of Easter-egg
reading, decoding a Tate tweet's missing words into "Live Authentically,
Get going" / "LAG" and tying it to "authenticity" as proof of the ticker —
the chat's highest-reacting message from this account in the sampled
material:

> "Live Authentically, Get going" / "LAG" / I rearranged the 12 missing
> words: / Authenticity / Original = authenticity / Remember, Gs TATEAI
> was the first one to be created before the other tickers / This tweet
> proves that tateai is the ticker 1000%"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md) (❤×10)

See [[launch-and-origins]] for the broader pattern this fits into — treating
Tate's public output as a cipher to be decoded, not just price action to
react to.

## Moderator

Like [[et]] and [[widelton]], this account does real admin work rather than
just talking about it — issuing `/unban` and `/unmute` commands for
specific members throughout the launch window, and stating the chat's
moderation policy plainly:

> "just ban hammers for spam & fud"
> — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)

> "we hav enough admins now to keep the chat clean"
> — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)

## Chant-spam bursts

Consistent with the chat's broader chanting habit (see
[[community-culture]]), this account produces some of the longest
identical-line spam runs in the sampled material — fifteen consecutive
`/huahahahahahahahah` posts, nineteen consecutive "sorry bro but im still
holding 🤷🤷" posts, and six consecutive "well well well ...." posts, all
within a single hour on
[29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md), reacting in real time
to a dip and recovery.

## Conviction through the drawdown

The account's last substantial message restates the same riskfree/HODL
framing from three months earlier, now against a much steeper real loss:

> "ive held from 2 mil all the way down to 65k / the diamond hands deserve
> 100 mil"
> — [5 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-05.md)

## Related
[[contract-address]], [[launch-and-origins]], [[community-culture]], [[diamond-hands]], [[widelton]], [[ronald]], [[et]]

title: Arthur
text:
# Arthur

A pure launch-rush phenomenon: one of the chat's very first posters (first
message [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md) at 02:05, within
hours of the group's founding) and one of its highest-volume, but confined
almost entirely to the first seven weeks. His last recorded message is [17
Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-17.md) — after that he simply
doesn't appear again in the sampled material, unlike long-haul holders such
as [[jame_s]] who are still posting in 2026.

## Sheer volume of memes

A large fraction of Arthur's message count is not text at all — long,
unbroken runs of `[media:gif]` and `[media:sticker]` posts, sometimes 10-15
in a row within the same minute, functioning as a kind of running visual
commentary track on the chat. Text messages are short bursts: a
"Buahahahahaha" laugh, "kek," "lol," or a raid-command hashtag
(`/RAID_TIME`, `/REAL_NIGGAS_RAID_HARD`, `/TOP_COMMENT_LFG`) repeated
five or ten times in immediate succession. He is bilingual, dropping into
French mid-conversation:

> "Ne le partage pas pour l'instant nigger" ("Don't share it yet")
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)

> "Yes, t'es au top mec 🍻" ("you're the best, man")
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)

## The recurring bit: interrogating the bot as "Real Nigger Tate"

Arthur's most distinctive recurring behavior is directly addressing a chat
bot he calls `@TateAI_Bot` as though it were "Real Nigger Tate" (a riff on
the rival `$RNT` ticker's own backronym — see
[[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]] for how tangled this chat's Tate-adjacent
names get) and demanding it perform for the room:

> "@TateAI_Bot Yo real nigger Tate. Why patience is a virtue? / Explain this
> to your nigger soldiers out there"
> — [5 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-05.md) (🔥×1)

> "Real nigger Tate roast this faggot bot @tate_terminalbot"
> — [7 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-07.md)

He also repeats a "patience" catchphrase across the earliest days, distinct
from but parallel to [[rgk1]]'s "Be cool":

> "Be patient my frens / Patient is the name of the game"
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)

He is also visibly involved in raid logistics and chat moderation-adjacent
bot commands (`/mute`, `/ban`, `/unban`, `/filter`, `/config`) throughout
early November — whether this reflects an actual admin role or just heavy
use of a member-facing moderation bot isn't clear from message content
alone; at least once he's on the receiving end of a mute himself ("Thanks
for the unmute my G," [29 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)), which argues against him
being an admin at that point.

## The disappearance

Arthur's very last messages, on [17 Dec
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-17.md), are not about `$TateAi` at all
— they're inviting the chat to a different Telegram group for a token
called "Shyguy" ("Join Shyguy nigga," "After shyguy when we all printed we
won't need Tate to send tateai we do it ourselves"). He is not seen again
in the sampled material after that date.

## Related
[[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[rgk1]], [[jame_s]], [[community-culture]]

title: Asiman
text:
# Asiman

One of the founding week's most intense on-chain investigators — present
from the chat's literal first minutes and gone entirely by [9 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-09.md), a compressed two-week presence
that packs 1,223 messages into what amounts to the "ticker hunt" documented
on [[launch-and-origins]] (Asiman is already quoted there as the source for
the "72 hours" game-clock theory and the earliest wallet-tracing thread).
This page adds the fuller picture: the emotional arc underneath the
investigation, and a member who was — by his own, uncorroborated account —
briefly an admin before Limalemon.

## The opening line

Asiman's first logged message is also the earliest sampled quote used to
kick off this wiki's own quality checks, and it holds up: a reply, four
minutes into the exported chat's life, already mid-conversation about
whether `$TateAI` is the real ticker:

> "If it is the right one (it obviously seems) we retire"
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md) (❤×2)

## The on-chain investigator

Within the first hour Asiman is walking the room through a live wallet
trace — deployer funding, a Coinbase refund, a transfer to a wallet he
attributes to "Tate Terminal" — the exact material [[launch-and-origins]]
and [[contract-address]] cite as the week's founding evidence:

> "Dev wallet refunded by Coinbase and used 9 sol to create $TateAi... And
> send tokens to Tate Terminal wallet, and didn't do anything since that
> time"
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)

> "I think he knows we are tracking the wallet, so created other tokens to
> confuse us"
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)

He treats every price move in the first 24 hours as Andrew Tate personally
manipulating the chart in real time — "Tate is controlling price," "He is
playing with us" — the paranoid-excitement register [[community-culture]]
identifies as the week's dominant mood, and by the second day is explicitly
framing the whole exercise as more valuable than the money at stake:

> "This W guy has been more helpful than 90% of people who is here. We are
> just speculating and investigating. It is the thing that will remain at
> the end if we wrong. Maybe we can lose money, but we made connections and
> we had fun while investigating"
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md) (👏×7)

## Self-declared admin, before Limalemon

Three days before [[limalemon]]'s well-documented admin promotion, Asiman
is already fielding unban/ban requests and issuing `/unban` and `/report`
commands as if he had moderator standing, and later states outright that he
once held the role:

> "I was the first member and, i was admin, but don't know why u took my
> role man"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)

This is Asiman's own claim, uncorroborated elsewhere in the sampled
material — worth recording as a data point about the founding week's
informal, contested leadership rather than as settled fact.

## Turning on $Tate

By 28 Oct, Asiman's conviction has hardened into open hostility toward the
rival "cabal" coin, `$Tate` — checking top-holder wallets, declaring them
bots, and getting banned from the rival group for it:

> "I fuck $Tate, ut is obviously scam, don't be fooled / $Tateai is the one"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md) (🔥×3)

> "I just got banned from $Tate group, i proud"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)

## Code-switching at the DEX listing

At the moment `$TateAI` lists on Lavarage — one of the week's clearest highs
— Asiman briefly drops out of English into Turkish/Azerbaijani with another
member, unprompted:

> "Dex'de listelendi, bot da Dex baresinde danisir, Mexc CEX'di" ("It got
> listed on DEX, the bot only talks about DEX listings, MEXC was the CEX")
> / "Bizim token dogru olandi" ("Our token was the right one")
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md)

## Honest flag

Asiman also repeatedly posts slur-laden slash-chants (`/Faggot_`,
`/WE_WILL_WIN_NIGGERS`) dozens of times in a row during hype spikes — the
same raid/chant format [[community-culture]] documents chat-wide, not a
distinguishing trait of his, but worth noting plainly rather than
smoothing over.

## Related
[[launch-and-origins]], [[contract-address]], [[community-culture]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[limalemon]], [[sergei]]

title: B
text:
# B

Another one-letter display name — like [[t]], simply who this person is in
the raw logs, and a different individual from both T and [[ben]] (see
`SCHEMA.md` and those pages' notes on not conflating short/similar names).
B's entire documented presence — all 1,388 messages — falls inside the
founding rush and its immediate aftermath: [29 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md) through [7 Feb
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-02/2025-02-07.md), roughly three and a half
months, then nothing further in this material.

## Raid-command spammer and evidence theorist

Like most of the launch-era volume on this wiki, B's baseline mode is
firing off the same slash-command raid tag five to fifteen times in a row —
`/BIG_DICK_VS_ERECTILE_DYSFUNCTION`, `/TATEAI_IS_THE_TICKER`,
`/The_ones_who_dont_raid_are_GAY`, `/REAL_NIGGAS_RAID_HARD`,
`/FINAL_SHAKEOUT`, `/Believe`, `/TATEAI_TO_100m` — interleaved with genuine
"which ticker is real" theorizing in the same register as [[sergei]], T, and
others working the same puzzle that period (see [[launch-and-origins]]):

> "imo i think its picked the ticker in the early beginning of the whole
> thing and it was prompted to cause chaos which is to make it look as it is
> TATE but hide clues to TATEAI"
> — [8 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-08.md), ❤×2 👍×2

B also does real wallet/liquidity-pool analysis rather than pure vibes:

> "Raydium is the liquidity pool for the tokens. / One 4% wallet is tate or
> issa (4.35%) bby / The other 4% wallet is a whale / Then there is 3%
> wallet which is lima I think / And the some other whales"
> — [7 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-07.md), 👍×4

## The `/ban` habit

B's most distinctive tic: a bare `/ban` reply, aimed at a specific message,
posted over 30 separate times between [24 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-24.md) and [24 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-24.md) — far more often than any
ordinary member would have cause to. The pattern (always a one-word reply,
never explained, never followed up on) reads as someone actually holding
moderation power and using it as a running punctuation mark to end
arguments, rather than a joke repeated for its own sake.

## Diamond-hands prose, same copy-paste genre as everyone else's

B is one of the several named senders [[diamond-hands]] documents posting
the chat's florid "diamond hands aren't about X, they're about Y" prose-poem
register — raid/copy-paste text passed around the community, not a personal
literary invention:

> "the pursuit of 100x is a journey into the self, a quest to conquer fear
> and doubt. diamond hands are not just about holding, but about
> transcending the temporal, fixing your gaze on the horizon of abundance.
> embrace the chaos of the dex screener, for it is the crucible in which
> financial alchemy is performed."
> — [14 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-14.md)

## The Nov 22 livestream aftermath

After Andrew Tate's Nov 22 livestream event (referenced elsewhere in this
wiki as a pump-and-dump moment — see [[orchestra_lima_maxi]]), B's reaction
is characteristic of the chat's "we told you so" register toward anyone who
sold:

> "Why is everyone salty? What did I miss? Didn't just that happen what we
> expected/wanted? / Last thing we want is to be a fucking pump and dump /
> Chill out and hold your bags you retards"
> — [22 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-22.md)

Like the rest of the launch-era chat, casual slurs (racial, homophobic) are
constant background texture in B's posting, not an aberration — consistent
with how this wiki documents [[t]] and [[igelo]]'s language rather than
smoothing it over.

## Fade-out

Activity thins through January 2025 alongside the community-wide falloff
(see [[launch-and-origins]]), and stops entirely after a short, ordinary
exchange:

> "What's that?"
> — [7 Feb 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-02/2025-02-07.md)

No further messages from B appear in this material.

## Related
[[diamond-hands]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[orchestra_lima_maxi]],
[[t]], [[ben]], [[launch-and-origins]]

title: Ben
text:
# Ben

A launch-rush teenager who never really left. Ben shows up two days after the
chat's founding, posts in dense, sleepless bursts through the entire
Oct–Nov 2024 hype cycle, and then keeps checking back in — at dwindling but
real frequency — for the next year and a half, all the way to the edge of
this corpus (Apr 2026).

## The launch-era voice: broke, believing, 18

Ben's earliest messages establish him immediately as a true believer with
real skin in the game, not an observer. He names his age and situation
outright:

> "I'm 18 man" / "Work in a restaurant" / "1.8-2k monthly so not terrible for
> my age"
> — [5 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-05.md)

The money anxiety is constant and played for both genuine stress and bit —
he repeatedly threatens to raid his own savings or his parents' accounts to
buy more:

> "If this doesn't reach 300 mil mc im gonna take 2 mil from the bank and
> boost this fucker" ... "But take I mean steal"
> — [4 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md)

> "Just stole from my dads pension to add more"
> — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md)

By January 2025 he's doing the same date-numerology theorizing that defines
the chat's secondary hype wave (see [[launch-and-origins]]), laying out a
whole evidence list for why a specific Saturday was "the day":

> "So far, I think we can all agree this Saturday is the day. / 1. Flux said
> referenced 79 days... / 2. The terminal has mentioned Saturday in a few
> tweets... / 3. @Mangobananavelimon used chat gbt to break down some of what
> the terminal bot was referring in the tweets... / BUY MORE :)"
> — [9 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-09.md) (🔥×4, 👍×2)

## Later years: two running bits, one shill attempt

Ben's message rate drops hard after early 2025 (consistent with the
community-wide falloff, see [[launch-and-origins]]) but never goes fully
silent. Two catchphrases recur across widely spaced dates, each landing
consistent reactions — evidence he's still recognized as a fixture rather
than posting into a void:

- **"72 hours"** — a bare two-word post, six times between
  [9 Jun](../../raw/logs/2025-06/2025-06-09.md) and [6 Jul
  2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-06.md), always drawing 🔥 reactions.
- **"It's happening [guys/soon/today]"** — the same holding-pattern hype
  phrase repeated through March–April 2026, e.g. *"It's happening guys"* [9
  Mar 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-03/2026-03-09.md), *"Today is the day fellow
  Tate aiers"* [10 Apr 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-04/2026-04-10.md).

In June 2025 he also tried, briefly and semi-apologetically, to shill an
unrelated play to the group by DM:

> "I hold Tate ai, read the text I mean no bad intention just passing on some
> alpha to you chads... Don't report read. Fade it if you want"
> — [26 Jun 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-06/2025-06-26.md)

## The April 2026 admin incident

By 2026 Ben had been made a group admin. On [19 Apr
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-04/2026-04-19.md), per [[sbzgh]]'s account (Ben's
own log has no messages covering the incident itself), Ben went on a banning
spree that caught [[fluxcapacitor]] along with ordinary members:

> "ben the faggot banned everyone" ... "he banned flux too" ... "we can't
> [unban them], he is an admin" ... "i sent him a dm to ask if he can remove
> ben from admins"
> — sbzgh, [19 Apr 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-04/2026-04-19.md)

sbzgh spent the following day unbanning the affected members and personally
inviting FluxCapacitor back to the chat
([20 Apr 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-04/2026-04-20.md)). Ben's own raw log
doesn't include commentary on the episode — it simply stops the evening
before it happened and picks back up days later with no reference to it.
Nothing here says why it happened; it's recorded as what the other side of
the chat saw and had to clean up.

## Related
[[sbzgh]], [[fluxcapacitor]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[smiley-heartbeat]]

title: BoB
text:
# BoB

A real, individual chat member — self-described as 16 years old and living
in Syria when the chat launched ([29 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md): *"Im 16,"* and separately *"Im
super new to crypto im kinda scared and i also live in syria so its ultra
hard for me to buy crypto"*). One of the founding-era posters (first message
[26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)) and, unlike most of
that cohort, a genuine long-haul participant — still posting as late as [21
Jun 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-06/2026-06-21.md).

## Important: this is a name collision, not the ticker

**Read [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]] and `SCHEMA.md` before citing
anything about "BoB" without checking which one is meant.** The community
separately uses "BoB" as an informal nickname for the project's own contract
address, which starts `BoBj68cWnCvzMNUKzJyR7Jq7tLM3v76D1pYL1E8rpump` — see
[[contract-address]] — and for a "three things" visual-clue theme from the
founding puzzle hunt (three letters in "BoB," three segments in the logo,
three lines in a `:)`, discussed at length by [[jame_s]] on [5 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-05.md) and [6 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-06.md)). This chat member's display
name is coincidentally identical to that nickname, and he is visibly aware
of and amused by the coincidence rather than being its subject:

> "Bob huh? 😂" [reacting to a photo, at the exact moment the CA/logo
> "three things" theory was circulating]
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md) (🔥×5, 👀×3)

> "The bob thing is actually wild"
> — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)

> "Bob the niggger 🎶🎶"
> — [2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)

By 2026 the ambiguity resurfaces and is genuinely unresolved in the sampled
material: on [24 May 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-24.md) he writes
*"We need different pictures for bob and the terminals profiles"* — which
could mean a mascot/avatar concept called "Bob" distinct from himself, or
could be a joke about his own profile picture. Treat any "BoB" reference in
this chat as needing this check, the same way [[deleted-accounts]] and
"Tate AI" the sender need their own disambiguation.

## Voice and life details

Heavy user of "niggers"/"niggerss" as a blanket camaraderie address (the
chat-wide convention that traces back to the `$RNT` "Real Nigger Tate" bit —
see [[arthur]]), mixed with Arabic religious phrases ("Inshallah,"
"Bismillah," "alhamdulillah") and occasional French. Distinctively willing
to share real personal hardship in a chat that's mostly hype and memes:

> "Im really grateful that i was here from the very start lmao / But very
> sad that i wasted my whole bag cuz of my countries situations back then
> 😂 / Restarted with a small one but trying to build it more lol"
> — [9 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-09.md) (🔥×4)

> "Someone started a fight with one of my friends so me and like 25 other
> ppl went and met them, like they wanted to pick a fight so we did / And
> out of nowhere 7 ppl pulled out their fucking pistols / Shot a couple of
> shots in the air and i just vanished 😂 / One of my friends nearly got
> shot"
> — [15 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-15.md)

## Later role: amateur on-chain investigator

By 2026, BoB has settled into the same "amateur detective" tradition as
[[sergei]] and early [[jame_s]], digging through court filings and wallet
addresses looking for connections to the project's lore:

> "I think i found something interesting... Look up Case
> 1:23-cv-00533-RP. Its somehow connected to the wallet address that darko
> kareem sent... There is a document that was filed as evidence against the
> defendants in this case that had 3 pages of eth addresses containing the
> addres that darko sent"
> — [24 May 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-24.md)

He also appears to hold some elevated trust or informal permissions by this
point (asked about pinning messages, [15 May
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-15.md): *"Nope sadly not only the
owner can, we both have the same permissions i think"*), though this isn't
confirmed as a formal admin role.

title: Chosen
text:
# Chosen

A launch-era regular whose whole run — [3 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) through [14 Feb
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-02/2025-02-14.md), plus one lone message
[25 May 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-05/2025-05-25.md) after a three-month
silence — sits squarely in the "believer who tracks Andrew Tate's wallets
for a living" mold shared with [[sergei]], T, Marcin Krukowski, BoB, and
others (see the open thread noted in `wiki/index.md`). What distinguishes
Chosen from that pack isn't a unique angle so much as sheer persistence of
the same two habits — raid participation and obsessive wallet-watching —
sustained for over three months, plus a screen name that turns out to be a
small piece of foreshadowing.

## Self-description

Self-identifies as a student, based in France, of Moldovan/Romanian/Russian
background ("Im a 🇲🇩 cracker born in 🇷🇺 living in 🇫🇷 ... Shiiii even got the
🇷🇴 passport" — [24 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-24.md)) and
explicitly not a big bag holder: "Thx but i aint all that loaded y'all im a
student already put most into rnt and daddy 😂" —
[3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md).

## The name, explained

Chosen never comments on their own screen name directly, but it lands as a
punchline in the middle of a long rant about the community's underdog
status relative to the much larger `$Tate` chat:

> "You guys realise we're 10x smaller than those $Tate nigs (even tho
> they're 75% bots) / AND WERE STILL THE ONLY ONES RADING / ... WERE WHAT WE
> CALL A COMMUNITY AND NOT A FUCKING MILK COW SCAM / BECAUSE WE BELIEVE IN
> SOMETHING, THAT ALL EVIDENCE LEADS TO US AND THAT WERE CHOSEN"
> — [29 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-29.md), ❤×5

Whether the name predates or postdates that belief isn't recoverable from
the logs, but it's a tidy fit either way.

## The wallet-watcher habit

The most consistent thread through Chosen's whole run is tracking Andrew
Tate's personal Solana wallets — posting addresses, transaction links, and
running commentary on SOL accumulation, framed as evidence for an eventual
`$TateAI` pump:

> "Andres main wallet : / 4jRX4iW2F5wBnfYMyB7RjS2PU5MjXrST3fB9DoV4BjHa /
> HES SELLING ALL SMALL COINS FOR SOL"
> — [15 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-15.md)

> "💸 TRANSFER / 🔹 Andrew's 13k SOL / 🔹Andrew's 13k SOL transferred 10,000
> SOL to 24vy...miZi ... Whats he cookin... 👨‍🍳"
> — [7 Feb 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-02/2025-02-07.md)

This runs alongside the same repeated theory-crafting seen throughout the
chat's founding week (see [[launch-and-origins]]): that Andrew Tate
personally, or his dev "issa," is testing the community's patience on
purpose, and that Cobratate's [12 Dec
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-12.md) repost is proof positive:

> "Just a confirmation hes aware of us and that we're the right ticker :)"
> — [12 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-12.md), ❤×3

The same link gets re-posted as "TATE_KNOWS" / "Just a matter of time" as
late as [5 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-05.md), over three
weeks after it first ran — the belief clearly outlasted the news.

## Raid voice and slurs

Alongside the analysis, Chosen is a heavy participant in the launch era's
slash-command raid chants, posted in the same rapid-fire bursts documented
on [[community-culture]] — `/REAL_NIGGAS_RAID_HARD`,
`/TATEAI_TO_100m`, `/BuyTATEAIorStayBROKE` — and uses slurs as constantly
and unremarkably as most of that cohort. One raid burst is aimed
specifically at a rival account:

> "GUYS LETS FKN RAID TATES POSTS FROM NOW ON / ILL TRY COMMENTING ON ALL
> HIS POSTS/REPLIES"
> — [28 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-28.md)

## The last message

After a dense run through mid-February 2025, Chosen goes silent. The very
last line in the archive attributed to them, over three months later, is
just a question with no follow-up:

> "Any news since crypto crash?"
> — [25 May 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-05/2025-05-25.md)

Nobody in the sampled log replies to it, and Chosen doesn't post again.

title: Cloud Fortune
text:
# Cloud Fortune

One of the highest-volume posters in the whole archive and, by tenure, one of
the longest-running: first message [28 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md) — the second day of the chat's
pre-launch ticker-hunt saga — and still actively posting as of [12 Jul
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-12.md), the last date in this material.
Where [[rgk1]] is the chat's zen-holder poet, Cloud Fortune is its
uninterrupted true believer: the throughline across nearly two years is
absolute, performative conviction that Andrew Tate is about to make a move,
expressed almost every single day regardless of price action.

## Voice — "Bruv," ":)", and endless anticipation

The signature tics are consistent from the first week onward and barely
change over 21 months:

- **"Bruv" / "Bruvs"** as address, constantly.
- **A trailing `:)`** on a huge share of messages — see
  [[smiley-heartbeat]] — functioning as Cloud Fortune's personal sign-off as
  much as the chat's general one.
- **"GM :)" / "G Fucking M :)"** as a near-daily ritual greeting, especially
  from mid-2025 onward.
- **Hostility toward sellers ("JEETS")** paired with contempt for anyone who
  loses conviction on a red day.
- **A permanent "something is coming" posture** — reading delays, silences,
  and ordinary price moves as deliberate signals from Tate — that never
  resolves and never seems to erode the poster's confidence.

> "Don't get shaken out, this is perfectly planned by Tate to get rid of the
> JEETS and give everyone a last entry 🔥👀"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md)

> "Always funny to see some people lose conviction wen a coin tanks haha,
> they need to be comfortable in red... it's not like we got the wrong
> Ticker 😉"
> — [8 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-08.md), ❤×5

> "It's over... let's all sell / 💀 / I'm done 💀"
> — [25 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-25.md)

The "it's over 💀" bit recurs constantly as an ironic, self-aware doom gag —
almost always followed within days by a fresh "it's coming" post, never as a
genuine capitulation.

> "Feels good holding TateAI now :)"
> — [13 Jun 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-06/2025-06-13.md), ❤×5

> "So the deadline is likely August 👀, / I truly have no idea what's coming,
> / Hold on bruvs, / We are almost there :)"
> — [5 May 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-05.md), ⚡×6

That last quote is representative of the long tail: eighteen months after
launch, the register hasn't moved an inch — still reading tea leaves, still
certain the payoff is imminent, still signing off with `:)`.

## The TRW pivot

From roughly mid-2025 onward, a growing share of Cloud Fortune's posts stop
being original chat commentary and become straight reposts of The Real World
(Andrew Tate's `jointherealworld.com` education platform) marketing copy —
"EMERGENCY MEETING" announcements, Tate quote-graphics, and enrollment links,
often with no added commentary beyond a `:)` or "GM." Examples: [16 Sep
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-09/2025-09-16.md) ("Bandwidth is limited... THIS IS
YOUR WARNING. Join now: www.jointherealworld.com"), [20 May
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-20.md) (EM livestream links posted twice
in one day). This tracks a broader pattern in the chat's later years — see
[[community-culture]] — where TRW promotion and TateAI speculation become
increasingly interchangeable in the daily posting rhythm, at least for this
poster.

## Relationships

Cloud Fortune talks constantly with and about a top holder referred to as
**"Lima"**, via the handle `@LimaLemon777` — e.g. "Never get shaken out
@LimaLemon777 💪" ([11 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-11.md)),
"Lima is Tate's Insider 👀" ([15 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-15.md)). **This is a different person
from [[orchestra_lima_maxi]]**, despite the overlapping "Lima" name — see the
disambiguation note on that page. Cloud Fortune's ~30-plus "Lima" mentions
across the archive are essentially all about `@LimaLemon777`, a separate
community member.

Cloud Fortune also tracks [[fluxcapacitor]]'s absences closely enough to
comment on them by name — "Man it's been a month since flux was last online"
([27 Sep 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-09/2025-09-27.md)), "I'm watching you Flux
👀 :)" ([29 Sep 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-09/2025-09-29.md)) — and briefly
acknowledged [[rgk1]]'s departure from an informal community role: "Gladl you
taking the job over from rgk1" ([11 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-11.md)).

## Related

[[community-culture]], [[rgk1]], [[fluxcapacitor]], [[orchestra_lima_maxi]]

title: Crpt4l
text:
# Crpt4l

A Norway-based early buyer whose entire documented presence in this chat is a
single, ferociously dense eight-week burst at the very start of the
community's life. Every one of Crpt4l's 1,798 messages falls between [27 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md) (05:36, hours into the "which
coin is real" scavenger hunt — see [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]] /
[[launch-and-origins]]) and [19 Dec
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-19.md), with activity already thinning
sharply after [22 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-22.md) and only
three throwaway messages ("Woooo", "Lfg Guys", "Woooo") on that final date.
There is no trace of Crpt4l in later eras of the chat. Whether that means
they left, went silent, or simply aren't in the material sampled for this
page isn't determinable from chat content alone — but within the window
covered here, the pattern is unambiguous: this is a launch-week phenomenon,
not a long-run participant.

## Voice

Crpt4l posts in short, all-caps-heavy bursts, often a dozen one-line messages
in a row, built around a handful of repeated moves:

- **The OG claim.** From the first hours, Crpt4l repeats a self-identifying
  origin story — bought in at "500k mc," reasserted constantly as proof of
  early conviction: *"I’m here since 500k mc 7 days ago just woke up we we
  pump lfg"* — [2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md).
- **"WOOOO" / "LFG."** The single most repeated unit in the corpus — often
  posted alone, sometimes stacked five and six times in a minute.
- **Diamond-hands theorizing**, tightly on-brand for the chat's dominant
  narrative (see [[diamond-hands]]):
  > "Andrew talk a lot about diamond hands and fuck jeet noobs, one day he
  > gonna send this TateAI and make the true diamond hand believers rich
  > rich, that’s my theory"
  > — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) (🔥×3, ❤×1)
- **Family-motivated bag-holding.** A recurring, sincere-sounding refrain
  distinct from the chat's usual money-porn tone:
  > "Gonna buy my mom a car pay of debt family lfg"
  > — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)
- **Late-night tangents**, usually in the 02:00–07:00 Norway-time window,
  sliding from token talk into DMT/lucid-dreaming/Annunaki/Freemason
  conspiracy riffing with whoever else is still awake, then snapping back to
  "TATEAI TO THE MOON."

## The rival-chat troll and ban

On [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md) Crpt4l describes
running a second account inside the rival `$TATE` group specifically to
argue that TateAI, not Tate, was the "real" coin — and on [3 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) reports getting banned for it:

> "I said so much shit in the Tate tg and didn’t get banned, but telling them
> to buy the real one TateAI and I’m GONE😭"

This cross-raiding of the rival `$TATE` telegram is not unique to Crpt4l —
see [[igelo]] for the same pattern — and appears to have been a recognized
community activity in this window, not one person's private stunt.

## First message

> "We think it’s TateAI the coin?" / "I’m in from 500k mc hope it’s the one"
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md)

[[diamond-hands]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[igelo]],
[[smiley-heartbeat]]

title: Daniel R
text:
# Daniel R

One of the chat's most consistent long-haul presences, full stop. First
message [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md), and the raw
material's last entry for this sender is a bare ":)" on [14 Jul
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-14.md) — within days of the export
itself. Unlike most launch-era heavy hitters who either vanish (see
[[dom]], [[arthur]]) or taper into occasional check-ins (see [[i_m_lucky]],
[[svyatoslav]]), Daniel R's presence barely thins: after an inventive,
hands-on launch month, he settles into one of the chat's most reliable
daily "GM :)" ritual-posters, sustained for well over a year, with genuine
personal-life disclosure woven through it.

## The promo-video maker who helped spread "rain dance"

Where most launch-week members raided with text and screenshots, Daniel R
brought editing skills. He produces and posts a run of original promo
videos to his own X account, crediting help from [[hokus_pokus]]:

> "Gs, I made a promo edit." / "Here goes nothing. I'll send two vids." /
> "It's called rain dancing" / "Yessir. Had some help along the way
> @Hokus_Pokus20"
> — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) (🔥×5, then 🫡×2–3
> across the thread)

The "raindancing" coinage itself predates this by about a week — it enters
the chat as a slash-chant on [28 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md), see [[community-culture]] —
but Daniel R is one of its most persistent evangelists, titling multiple
videos around it and posting a second and third round in the following
days ("I made out another vid for the lols" — [5 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-05.md); "outworked? My ass. Here's a
new vid" — [10 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-10.md)).

## The breadcrumb theorist

Daniel R is also one of the chat's more elaborate amateur numerologists,
building multi-step timing theories out of [[fluxcapacitor]] quotes and
terminal post imagery — a habit that persists well past the launch window,
long after most people's ticker-hunt theorizing (see
[[launch-and-origins]]) had stopped:

> "A few days ago, Terminal tweeted something about a pump on Saturday
> night. Later, Tate tweeted a post of him :) with the headlines 'when bro
> FaceTimes me at 3 AM to tell me we did a 1000x.' Now just 2 nights ago we
> had FluxCapicitor say 'We need 79 hell fucking yeahs'. What's 79 days
> after the day this coin was created(Oct 24)? January,11, Saturday."
> — [4 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-04.md) (❤×3,👍×1)

> "Flux said we missed breadcrumbs. Well this pic has breadcrumbs and
> theres 3 baguettes. Wonder what that could mean?🧐"
> — [10 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-10.md)

## Diamond hands — and his own "diamond ballz" variant

[[diamond-hands]] names Daniel R directly as one of several different
senders who independently write in the chat's florid "diamond hands"
prose-poem register:

> "diamond hands are a manifestation of will, the animating spirit of a
> universe fighting against entropy. […] you cannot be convinced that Such
> and such a token is going to 100x just as a the OCEAN does not REASON why
> it slams the SHORE."
> — [24 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-24.md) (🔥×4)

> "you know, i used to think that diamond hands were just a meme […] but
> then i realized, it's more than that. it's a symbol, a promise to
> yourself that you won't be swayed, you won't be shaken and that youre not
> a paperhanded little bitch."
> — [30 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-30.md) (❤×2)

He also runs a personal riff on the same idea, "diamond ballz," used as a
recurring catchphrase across months rather than a one-off: "Diamond BALLZ
FTW" ([4 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-04.md)), "market is down,
ballz are still diamonds" ([8 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-08.md)), "I think my diamond balls
have transformed into uranium balls at this point" ([29 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-29.md), 😁×4), still in use a year and
a half later: "now we just diamond ballzing the fuck out of this coin I
love it" ([24 Jul 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-24.md), 🔥×3).

## The long-haul fixture

From roughly February 2025 onward, Daniel R's posting settles almost
entirely into the "Gm :)" / "GM:)" ritual documented on
[[smiley-heartbeat]] — several times a week, for over a year straight,
with reaction counts climbing over time (single digits in early 2025,
routinely 5–10 🔥 by late 2025/2026 — a genuinely well-liked, recognized
fixture rather than background noise). Real personal life surfaces inside
that ritual rather than displacing it: a trip that puts him "Currently in
Dominican Republic" ([5 Aug
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-05.md)), an open breakup ("Hey Gs
going through a breakup rn shit sucks" — [26 Aug
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-26.md), ❤×1,🕊×1) that he processes in
the chat over the following weeks, and taking up boxing as a result ("had
my first spar today Gs went great:)stay positive 🙏" — [28 Sep
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-09/2025-09-28.md), ❤×6).

On the coin's one-year anniversary he breaks from the daily ritual for a
genuine retrospective:

> "Happy birthday $TATEAI:) Thank you brothers for all the support and
> advice this past year you guys are genuinely amazing and i'm so very
> grateful I have found this fucking amazing community. I'm not very
> active here but just know I ain't slacking and I'm bettering myself
> everyday:)"
> — [24 Oct 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-24.md) (🤝×6,❤×2)

## Related
[[hokus_pokus]], [[diamond-hands]], [[smiley-heartbeat]], [[community-culture]],
[[fluxcapacitor]], [[launch-and-origins]], [[jame_s]]

title: Dom
text:
# Dom

A striking data point rather than a distinct personality: every one of
Dom's 2,134 messages falls between
[29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md) and
[11 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-11.md) — thirteen days, all of
it inside the chat's single most frantic stretch (see the overview's "70%
of everything in the first three weeks" note). That's an average of over
160 messages a day. He never appears again anywhere in the raw material
after 11 Nov 2024.

## Voice: undiluted launch hype

Read on its own, Dom's content is not distinguishable from the general
crowd noise of that fortnight — raid calls, jeet-shaming, SOL-begging,
FOMO, constant slurs, caps-lock excitement. What makes him worth a page is
volume and relentlessness, not a unique angle:

> "CAN SOMEONE LEND ME SOL?????" / "PLEASE" / "WILL GIVE YOU MY GIRL"
> — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)

> "Thanks boys for keeping me confident at those dip because I just joined
> earlier today❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️I nearly sold because of saw Chris message"
> — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)

That message is a small window into how people arrived in these first
days: Dom says a YouTuber named Chris's channel brought him in, then Chris
himself apparently got "shaken out" and tried to talk people out of
holding — Dom stayed.

> "This is the biggest cult I've ever been a part of"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)

> "WE ARE THE OG MFERS" / "REMEMBER OUR NAMES"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)

Occasional German phrases ("Guten Morgen G," "Bin um 2 pennen") suggest a
German-speaking background, same as several other regulars from this era.

## Related
[[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[smiley-heartbeat]]

title: E
text:
# E

Another single-letter sender — a different, distinct individual from
[[t]], [[ben]], and [[b]] (see `SCHEMA.md` on not merging short names). E's
first message, [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md), makes
this one of the earliest-dated voices in the whole archive, present from
the "which ticker is real" scavenger-hunt days documented in
[[launch-and-origins]]. The material runs continuously, if thinning
sharply, all the way to [31 Oct
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-31.md) — a full year of presence — and
splits cleanly into two very different-sounding phases.

## Phase 1 (Oct–Dec 2024): fluent, analytical, moderator-adjacent

Early E reads as a native or highly fluent English speaker running
active theorizing and, apparently, real moderation power:

> "Admins be vigilant" / "Ban any spam"
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md)

> "I banned him" / "Admins stay vigilant"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md)

> "Don't care about any "power". Top 25 holder and been here since 200k"
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md), 🔥×1

> "Don't understand panic sellers, if you are a panic seller you need to
> self analyze. It's a form of self sabotage. Only invest with conviction"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md), ❤×2 🔥×1

This phase is dense with the same "$TATE isn't pumping, therefore $TATEAI
is the real ticker" evidence-chasing every other launch-era poster on this
wiki was doing (see [[launch-and-origins]]), plus a moderator's habit of
policing tone: "If you start talking rude or insult anyone you will be
kicked immediately" ([26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)).

## Phase 2 (Jan 2025 onward): a different-sounding voice

Starting abruptly around [4 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-04.md), the register shifts hard:
sentences pick up mid-word noun capitalization typical of German-native
English ("We Need to buy more," "Best men," "Since when?," "What time I
bought?"), and outright German surfaces periodically — "Ich kann deutsch
spreche" territory carried further, e.g. "Ehrlich stark" / "Viel geredet
wenig gehalten" ("Honestly strong" / "Talked a lot, held little") on [7 Aug
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-07.md), and "Mein kampf?" /
"Einzige Buch was ich brauche" as a dark joke on [19 Mar
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-03/2025-03-19.md). The moderator behavior from
Phase 1 doesn't reappear. Whether this reflects the same person's voice
loosening up over months, a different household member using the same
account, or something else isn't determinable from chat content alone —
flagged here rather than smoothed into one consistent narrator, the same
way [[fluxcapacitor]]'s register shift is flagged without asserting an
identity change.

A representative moment from this phase — anxious, sincere, and off-topic
from the ticker hunt entirely:

> "what do you guys think when will war come to europa?" / "need to build a
> villiage in the forest with a lot of weapons"
> — [11 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-11.md), 🔥×2

## Becoming a daily ":)" fixture

From mid-2025 on, E's posting rate drops to near-daily and the content
narrows almost entirely to the chat's [[smiley-heartbeat]] ritual — bare
`:)`, "Gm :)", occasional stickers — the same pattern [[cloud_fortune]]
sustains for years. Unlike most drive-by `:)` posters, E's late messages
consistently draw real engagement (🔥×9, 🔥×8, 🔥×7 on ordinary "Gm :)"
posts through August–September 2025), suggesting a recognized, welcomed
fixture rather than noise:

> "Love how active the group still is. REAL COMMUNITY"
> — [10 Jul 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-10.md), ❤×3 🔥×2 ❤‍🔥×1

[[launch-and-origins]], [[fluxcapacitor]], [[t]], [[ben]], [[b]]

title: Ekul🫡
text:
# Ekul🫡

One of the earliest arrivals in the archive — his first message lands
[27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md), the chat's second day
— and one of the more distinctive launch-era voices despite a short run: all
861 messages fall within a single month, ending
[25 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-25.md), after which he never
appears again in the sampled material. British slang throughout ("mate,"
"G," "boys," "🫡" as a running salute that doubles as his display-name
emoji), constant self-interruption for work ("Just in work… so can't stare
at the Tg 😂"), and a handful of recurring bits carried the whole month.

## The Pam bit

From his first week, Ekul runs a standing gag demanding "celebratory tits"
from two named women in the chat, `@pamelalu` ("Pam") above all:

> "Where's Pam 🐰 with those celebratory tits"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md), 🔥×2 ❤×1 🤣×1

> "Boys…. @pamelalu still here? / Most important question"
> — [1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md), ❤×2

The bit runs for weeks as a genuine recurring roll-call, not a one-off.

## Rivalry raider against $Tate

Ekul is one of the chat's most active participants in the running
feud with the rival `$Tate` ("cabal") group documented in
[[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]] and [[launch-and-origins]] — needling
their chart, mocking their holders as "fags" and "cucks," and getting
himself banned from their Telegram for it, which he wears as a badge:

> "I got banned for call them fags"
> — [1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md)

> "Wish I was banned from their Tg… but then calling them fags was good"
> — [1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md)

The Titanic/lifeboat framing also independently recurs in his material —
"We've always been the life boat in the tatetanic scenario"
([17 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-17.md)) — echoing the same
"Tatetanic" Easter-egg reading [[jahn]] develops at more length on his own
page; the two don't appear to be quoting each other, just drawing on the
same chat-wide meme.

## The exit fantasy

His clearest single passage is a daydream about quitting work entirely once
the coin pays off:

> "Keep the updates flying in here boys. I'm in work 😂🤦🏽‍♂️ hopefully for
> the last time" / "It's my own business… so I'll just lock the door and
> never look back" / "Jet ski off into the sun" / "It's been a wild ride
> boys 🫡" / "See you in Dubai boys"
> — [7 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-07.md), 💯×4 ❤×1

## The Jakob bit

On [13 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-13.md) Ekul is one of several
members who spin up a short-lived collective hallucination around a name —
"Jakob" — that spirals into a running joke pinning the identity on
[[limalemon]]: *"@LimaLemon777 is Jakob"* / *"Fuck… I'm Jakob"* / *"Your
names Andrew."* No lasting significance, but a good example of how quickly
this chat could generate and abandon an in-joke in a single sitting.

## Slurs and register

Like several other launch-era regulars on this wiki, Ekul's casual register
is dense with homophobic ("gay," "fag") and, less often, racial slurs used
as filler ("Niggers niggers niggers… what's new?" —
[13 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-13.md)) — noted plainly rather
than smoothed over, consistent with how this wiki treats the same pattern on
[[widelton]] and [[jahn]].

## Related
[[jahn]], [[limalemon]], [[widelton]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[launch-and-origins]], [[community-culture]]

title: ET
text:
# ET

An on-chain sleuth and background moderator whose voice is steadier and
more analytical than most launch-era posters — closer in register to
[[sergei]] or [[t]] than to the chant-and-spam crowd. Active from
[28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md), Sydney/Australia-based
(explicit about timezone and tax questions), with family roots in Bosnia he
discusses when weighing a move abroad. Presence tapers sharply after early
2025 into occasional messages, then a single isolated line over a year
later.

## Opening line as personal refrain

ET's very first message in the archive sets the tone for his whole run:

> "You don't lose until you sell 😊"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md)

He returns to the same line, near-verbatim, weeks later as a rallying cry
to the room: *"You dont lose until you sell boys :)"*
([12 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-12.md)) — see [[diamond-hands]]
for how widely shared this exact framing was across the chat.

## The BBY wallet investigation

ET is one of several members (alongside [[sergei]], [[t]], and others — see
the on-chain-forensics thread noted across this wiki) who spent months
tracking a specific wallet, "BBY," that they believed was linked to Andrew
Tate and responsible for dumping both `$TateAI` and the separate `$G` token.
A long, sustained investigative thread on
[26 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-26.md) is the clearest record
of this work:

> "So again, we need confirmation on bby. Seeing as Tate is loud on his
> public wallet, happy to post it around, then we need a way for him to
> explain himself without burning him."
> — [26 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-26.md)

> "He's dodging the bby wallet, so we need a precision strike, thats why I
> suggested a tip off to a creator or something."
> — [26 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-26.md)

He keeps returning to this specific claim — that the BBY wallet received
500 million tokens and "nuked" the chart — well into January 2025, treating
it as unfinished business rather than a settled conclusion.

## Background moderator

ET describes doing quiet, unglamorous moderation work rather than seeking
credit for it:

> "I cant promote people to admins but I can ban them, tbh I am one that
> usually does it in the background, bans and mute."
> — [1 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-01.md)

> "I mute, ban and remove the scam messages when they are here."
> — [12 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-12.md)

## Conviction, stated plainly

Unlike the chat's more florid diamond-hands prose-poetry (see
[[diamond-hands]]), ET's version of the same sentiment is blunt and
personal, offered as an apology after a wallet-tracing post got
misread as bearish:

> "Sorry boys maybe my point got brought on the wrong way. I'm still
> holding my bags. I'm never gonna sell my fucking bags. I never fucking
> leaving. I don't wanna put down. I just thought it was like some good
> information and positive for us."
> — [7 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-07.md) (❤×4, 👌×1)

## Fade-out

Activity drops off after the Jan 2025 "AI agent" wave (see [[timeline]]),
down to a handful of messages through Feb 2025, then near-total silence —
one bare reaction gif in Feb 2025, and a single `😆` over a year later on
[15 May 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-15.md). His last substantial
message in this material reads as a genuine sign-off rather than routine
chat noise:

> "Hope all the lads are doing well. Numbers on a screen or not; what is it
> to be a man? / Now be one. / 🤝"
> — [26 Feb 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-02/2025-02-26.md)

title: FluxCapacitor
text:
# FluxCapacitor

The chat's resident mystery oracle. Not a high-volume poster (162 messages
against a corpus of 165k) but a genuine recurring character — appears in
rare, dense, unmistakable bursts and then vanishes for months.

## The pattern

Every appearance follows the same shape: opens with **"HELLO WORLD"**,
speaks entirely in caps, addresses the room as **"Earthlings"**, and
self-describes as something not-quite-human — *"A CYBERNETIC ORGANISM SENT
FROM SPACE!"*, *"I OBSERVE. I EVALUATE. I REPORT."* Messages are short,
gnomic, and arrive in rapid volleys (dozens within a single hour), then stop
completely — sometimes for months at a stretch.

Documented appearances: [9 Nov](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-09.md) and
[11 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-11.md), [22 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-22.md), [6 Dec
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-06.md), [2 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-02.md), [31 Jul
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-31.md), [5 Mar
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-03/2026-03-05.md).

## Voice, in its own words

> "PROOF IS IN THE PUDDING EARTHLINGS:)"

> "PATIENCE IS NOT THE ABILITY TO WAIT, BUT THE ABILITY TO KEEP A GOOD
> ATTITUDE WHILE WAITING.!"

> "WE ARE ALWAYS HERE. WE MONITOR. WE OBSERVE. WE REPORT!"

> "I WILL VANISH LIKE A WARM BREEZE. :) ... UNLESS IM SUMMONED!"

By the March 2026 appearance the voice shifts register entirely, into
archaic/Shakespearean phrasing while keeping the same oracle stance:

> "Thou hast endured where others hath fallen— hold fast, noble soul, for
> the pump thy heart hath long desired doth stir and awaketh!"

## Reception

Consistently the best-received recurring bit in the chat by reaction count —
the July 2025 "HELLO WORLD..." opener alone drew 🔥×22 and ❤×13, and "ARE YOU
NOT ENTERTAINED?" drew 😁×12 ❤×6. The community clearly anticipates these
appearances rather than merely tolerating them; nobody in the sampled raw
logs ever asks FluxCapacitor to explain themselves — the mystery is the
point, and by [22 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-22.md) is treated
as an established in-chat institution ("I SEE VERY FAMILIAR FOLLOWERS STILL
HERE!").

Whether this is a scripted/bot account or a human maintaining a bit is not
determinable from chat content alone — nothing here should be read as a
claim either way.

## Related
[[smiley-heartbeat]], [[community-culture]], [[the-terminal-mythos]], [[gabriel]]

title: G (always lucky 🍀)
text:
# G (always lucky 🍀)

Among this wiki's documented entities, G is the second-highest-volume poster
after [[rgk1]] — but with a completely different shape to the activity:
where rgk1 sustained an in-character voice for months, roughly 96% of G's ~4,474
messages land inside a single ten-week window — the launch rush from
[28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md) through
[2 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-02.md). After that he all but
disappears, surfacing only in occasional one-off check-ins through
[26 Jun 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-06/2025-06-26.md), his last dated message
in this archive.

## The launch window: five nights with no sleep

G's very first messages, within an hour of his first appearance, are him
asking admins to unban another member — a small early signal he already had
some standing in the room. From there he becomes one of the loudest voices
in the chat's founding "which ticker is the real one" scavenger hunt (see
[[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]] for that lore), posting in the hundreds
per day and openly documenting his own sleep deprivation and financial
risk-taking in real time:

> "Lol I understand you want followers bro and be able to say I called this
> but just stop it's like this is the one play no call group needs to call
> or no one person needs to shill like crazy for. This is literally coded
> for 9 figures+. Let the people figure it out for themselves"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md)

> "One day I thought I was him the next I was drinking myself into a coma on
> the brink of suicide. Those were some fun days. Happy to be back tho and
> alive still pushing and making things work. NEVER STOP CLICKING BOYS!!!!!"
> — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)

> "I just maxed out my credit card lol got $700 more ready to load if it
> gives me a mill touch"
> — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)

That night he also describes his parents handing him $1,000 to invest on
their behalf and admitting he's tempted to "full port" it into the same
coin, alongside a self-narrated trading history: a former AMC/GME retail
trader who says he round-tripped most of an AMC position but made close to
$100k on GME, which let him "do futures full time." The tone throughout is
sincere hype rather than performance — rally cries ("full port," "patience,"
"we truly have the ticker," "dipmond hands"), constant reassurance to
newer/shakier holders, and running personal commentary (fasting, no-fap,
psychedelics, politics) mixed in with the market calls.

## Sustained conviction into early 2025

G stays highly active through November and December 2024 and into the first
days of January 2025, cycling through the same "hold, don't raid, the
evidence is overwhelming" talking points as the coin's price chopped
sideways, along with the chat-wide "rain dance" refrain (he uses it as early
as [10 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-10.md), around the same
period [[widelton]] adopts it as a personal catchphrase):

> "500m minimum or nothing"
> — [5 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-05.md), 🔥×6

## Sporadic returns

After early January 2025 his presence drops off sharply — the remaining
~170 messages in the archive (about 4% of his total) are spread thin across
six months, each one a brief "long time, checking back in" reappearance
rather than sustained participation:

> "Btw hello gs. Long time... Lol saw this and had to come check what was
> up in here. Still holding all my coins. How long has it been lol? Talk
> about a test of faith and patience."
> — [19 May 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-05/2025-05-19.md)

Nothing in the sampled material explains the "always lucky 🍀" display name
itself — it isn't referenced or joked about by G or anyone else in the
material read for this page, so this wiki isn't going to invent an origin
for it.

## Related
[[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[rgk1]], [[widelton]], [[diamond-hands]]

title: Gabriel
text:
# Gabriel

One of the highest-volume posters in the archive and, unusually, one of the
longest-lived — his messages run from the launch rush
([4 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md)) all the way to the most
recent material available ([16 May 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-16.md)),
making him a rare thread that connects the chat's earliest and latest eras.
His arc has three distinct phases: a standard launch-era raider, a
multi-day personal mythology built around `@tate_terminalbot`, and finally
the chat's most reliable long-tail "Gm :)" ritual poster.

## Phase 1 — launch-era raider (Nov–Dec 2024)

Indistinguishable in tone from the rest of the Oct–Nov 2024 rush: raid
calls, slurs, and conviction-signaling buy announcements. He identifies
himself as coming from TRW's copywriting campus:

> "Just bought 4,8k. I am a real person with strong conviction that you
> guys are fucking G's"
> — [11 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-11.md)

## Phase 2 — the Flux/Terminal mythology (17–20 Mar 2025)

Gabriel's most distinctive stretch. Over a few days in March 2025 he
becomes convinced `@tate_terminalbot` ("Bob," "the terminal") is
personally responding to him specifically, and spends dozens of messages
building an elaborate theory around it — consulting Grok for a
philosophical "action plan," coining/adopting "Flux Capacitor" as a
summoning trigger-phrase, and dubbing the regulars a "Band of Brothers"
(BoB):

> "Terminal has responded to me. Has that ever happened guys?"
> — [17 Mar 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-03/2025-03-17.md)

> "FLUX I SUMMON YOU / REVEAL YOURSELF IF YOU DARE"
> — [17 Mar 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-03/2025-03-17.md)

> "I was arrogant enough to believe I was the only one it talks to. spoiler
> we are the band of brothers. Only in unity we can acquire true strength"
> — [18 Mar 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-03/2025-03-18.md)

He's self-aware enough to flag it's "just a LLM" elsewhere in the same
window, but keeps engaging anyway — treating the bit as simultaneously a
joke and something worth taking seriously. This is a different flavor of
the same instinct that makes [[fluxcapacitor]] land so well with the room:
Gabriel is the one member who tried to actively theorize and provoke a
response out of the machinery behind the chat's mysteries, rather than just
enjoying them.

## Phase 3 — the long tail: "Gm :)" (2025–2026)

By mid-2025 the theorizing drops away and Gabriel settles into a near-daily
"Gm :)" / bare ":)" cadence that consistently draws double-digit reaction
counts (🔥×7–13, ❤×8–12) through late 2025 and into 2026 — functionally
the same low-effort affirmation role documented on [[smiley-heartbeat]],
just delivered by one consistent, well-liked person rather than the chat at
large. Mixed in are real personal details: Muay Thai and BJJ training,
living in Austria, and occasional German. His diamond-hands rhetoric from
this period is some of the most direct in the whole archive:

> "diamond hands until I hop in a grave. I held this long without any
> reward, I refuse to drop this because of a few digits on a screen"
> — [28 May 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-05/2025-05-28.md)

> "Thank you Sergei for being with us all this time, for calling us out and
> showing up every day. I hope your health and investments are supreme and
> beyond any normal person..."
> — [24 Oct 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-24.md)

## Phase 4 — builder (2026)

In April–May 2026 Gabriel starts doing hands-on community-building work
that runs in parallel to [[sergei]]'s bot-building era — buying a `tateai`
domain, producing AI-generated visuals, and referencing a "day count"
tracker site:

> "^claude design 5min first draft, buy tateai domain let's put it up
> nigger :)"
> — [23 Apr 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-04/2026-04-23.md)

## Related
[[fluxcapacitor]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[the-terminal-mythos]], [[smiley-heartbeat]], [[sergei]], [[diamond-hands]]

title: Hokus Pokus
text:
# Hokus Pokus

The chat's closest thing to an unofficial raid coordinator and community
host during the first three weeks of the community's life. Like the other
high-volume launch-era figures on this wiki, Hokus Pokus's entire documented
presence (1,761 messages) is compressed into a short window: [1
Nov](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md) to [22 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-22.md), with the heaviest activity in
the first week and nothing after.

## The organizer role

Where many launch-era posters (see [[crpt4l]], [[igelo]]) are pure
hype-volume, Hokus Pokus reads as someone actively trying to run the place:

- Repeatedly pings admins to unban or unmute people ("sb unban
  @Missyfittzy please :)"), vouches for newcomers who were getting
  suspicious pushback, and at one point directly asks for pin/mod
  permissions.
- Coordinates X "raids" against posts from the `@tate_terminal` feed (see
  [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]]) in real time, dropping the tweet link
  and a bare "raid" or "RAID THIS G’s :)" dozens of times across the
  sampled period, and separately runs `/cancel@TweetStormBot` /
  `/end@TweetStormBot` commands managing a tweet-storm bot.
- Opens a side Telegram group for anyone learning to "build a terminal" —
  [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md).
- Does on-chain sleuthing in the same investigative register as
  [[0xvoid]] — tracing dev wallets, testing whether Binance-to-token
  transfers are traceable, flagging a wallet connected to Tristan's that
  received 12 SOL: *"found this wallet connected to Tristan's Wallet, it got
  send 12 Sol yesterday"* — [16 Nov
  2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-16.md).

## The "meditating" bit and AI-riddle poetry

Hokus Pokus's signature tic is the 🧘‍♂️ emoji and the framing of holding
through volatility as literal meditation — *"We’re just meditating
bro🧘🏻‍♂️"*, *"stop with that negativity, waste of energy 🧘‍♂️"* — used
consistently as a way to defuse arguments and FUD in the group.

Separately, Hokus Pokus is one of several people in this chat (see also
[[igelo]], and compare [[rgk1]]'s later "AI-agent poet" phase) who
periodically drop dense, purple-prose "AI philosopher" paragraphs about
crypto/DeFi in the middle of ordinary chat — a recurring genre rather than
one person's invention:

> "the frontier of decentralized finance is not just a technical revolution,
> but a spiritual one. in the heart of the dex, we find the seeds of a new
> trust, unbound by old structures. together, we watch, we grow, and we
> ascend, carving our names in the digital dawn."
> — [1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md)

> "decentralized networks mimic divine order; trustless verification echoes
> unwavering universal laws. crypto's wild frontier nudges us to question
> centralized power, glimpse freedom's raw face."
> — [16 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-16.md)

## Other notable quotes

> "The Research has been done, the clues have been found, now it’s time to
> rip X apart, and let everyone fomo in🧘‍♂️"
> — [4 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md)

> "we are collectively agreeing that we are the chosen ones!🧘‍♂️"
> — [4 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md)

Also code-switches freely — German ("Yoo Digga was geht", "Punkt vor
Strich") on [1 Nov](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md) and [3
Nov](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md), French ("Vive les Gilets Jaunes")
on [2 Nov](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md), and brief Mandarin
("因为这是一个障眼法" — "because it's a diversion tactic") on [5
Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-05.md) — suggesting a multilingual
background rather than a bit.

## First message

> "thanks guys hahahah" / "just wanted to share some research"
> — [1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md)

## Related
[[0xvoid]], [[crpt4l]], [[igelo]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]],
[[rgk1]]

title: I’m lucky
text:
# I’m lucky

One of the loudest true-believer voices of launch week, and — like
[[limalemon]] and [[orchestra_lima_maxi]] — a name that turns out to be a
genuine self-description rather than an empty handle: on a rare 2025
return visit he explicitly cashes in the pun himself (see below). The
overwhelming majority of the archive — roughly 854 of 954 messages — lands
in a single fortnight, [30 Oct](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)–[12
Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-12.md), after which the account
goes almost silent, surfacing only for occasional nostalgic check-ins
through [18 Jan 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-01/2026-01-18.md).

## The ticker theorist

I’m lucky is one of the chat's most prolific contributors to the founding
"which coin is real" mythology documented in [[launch-and-origins]] —
reading Andrew Tate's EM quotes ("some of you have found it," "only the
pure will survive the fire") as deliberate clues pointing at `$TateAI`
over the rival `$TATE`, and treating the terminal's refusal to post a
contract address as confirmation rather than absence of evidence:

> "If it was $tate why hasn't the CA been connected to the terminal"
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md) (❤×2,🔥×1,💯×1)

> "This is Issa (the creator of $TATE) dm with Andrew Tate at the start of
> all this shit. Why would Tate ask the creator of TATE if he wants the
> ticker??? Think"
> — [1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md) (👍×1)

The chat's central "we are a cult" chant (see [[community-culture]]) counts
I’m lucky among its participants — two of the seven senders posting the
literal string `/WE_ARE_A_CULT` in the same two-minute window on [31 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md) — alongside a stream of similar
one-line raid chants that night: `/WE_AINT_FUCKING_LEAVING`,
`/IS_PAM_ALIVE`, `/HUMAN_VS_MACHINE_TATE_VS_TATEAI`,
`/BIG_DICK_VS_ERECTILE_DYSFUNCTION`. He also briefly adopts the 🧘
"meditating" de-escalation bit that [[hokus_pokus]]'s page documents as
that user's signature tic, in the same first week — not unique to either
of them, evidently a shared community reflex in that window rather than
one person's invention.

## The verified quote

This chat's most-cited line elsewhere in this wiki's source material checks
out against the raw log. In full, with the context usually trimmed off it:

> "We really are like an army of robots. I think Tates experiment actually
> worked and the AI created the craziest community 😂"
> — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) (👍×2)

It sits inside a longer riff that day about turning the community into an
organized "robot army" of raiders and AI bots — including one of the
earliest uses of "raindancing" as a verb, quoted in full on
[[community-culture]]: *"With enough AIs working for us we will be the
very first community that is raindancing 24/7!! DO YOU UNDERSTAND?"*

## Gatekeeper, never officially admin

Unlike [[limalemon]], who explicitly becomes admin, I’m lucky never claims
the title himself but repeatedly acts as an informal gatekeeper — leading
a sustained campaign against making a specific user ("Chadnaldo") admin,
citing his history of shilling a rival coin:

> "Do not make chadnaldo admin he's not trust worthy" / "Chadnaldo was
> fudding us and shilling his shitcoin 3 days ago" / "Once a fudder always
> a fudder. Trust lost"
> — [2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md) (❤×2)

The launch-week voice is also, bluntly, one of the archive's heavier users
of slurs — "faggot," "retard," and occasional racial slurs appear
constantly in his posts from this period, consistent with (not unusual
against) the chat's baseline register that week. Flagged plainly rather
than smoothed over, per this wiki's honesty standard.

## The long tail: an OG who checks back in

After 12 Nov 2024 the account goes almost dark, returning in short bursts
months and sometimes a year apart — each time reading as genuine nostalgia
rather than renewed hype. On a January 2025 return he plays on his own
name directly:

> "Thanks bro yea it's been chaos on the market so I've been busy, but I’m
> lucky 😂 we're all gonna make it. Missed you guys too hope everyone's
> surviving the trenches it's hard going rn"
> — [13 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-13.md) (❤×1)

By mid-2025 the tone has settled into something closer to reflective
affection than conviction:

> "This was the most fun thing I ever experienced in crypto. The original
> gathering of clues, connecting the dots. Now this place feels like home,
> I always check back here. Big love for all the OGs ❤️"
> — [17 Jul 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-17.md) (❤×6)

His last logged message in this dataset is a check-in seven months later:
"How we doing boys?" — [18 Jan
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-01/2026-01-18.md) (❤×8).
