title: IGelo
text:
# IGelo

A high-volume launch-week chanter whose style overlaps heavily with
[[crpt4l]] — connect-the-dots readings of Terminal tweets, slash-command
chant spam, and heavy use of slurs as in-group filler — but with a
distinctive tic of their own: a bare ✅ dropped after nearly any confident
claim. Like the other figures on this wiki, IGelo's entire documented
presence (1,637 messages) is a short, front-loaded burst: [27 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md) to [10 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-10.md), roughly two weeks, with nothing
after.

## Voice and recurring bits

- **"We are on the right ship"** — repeated near-verbatim across the whole
  window as a rallying line, e.g. [28
  Oct](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md), [29
  Oct](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md), [4
  Nov](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md).
- **Slash-command chant spam**, the same device used across this chat's
  early days: `/TateAi_THIRD_CRYPTO`, `/DIAMOND_HANDS`, `/RAINDANCE`,
  `/5M_MARKETCAP_24H` fired a dozen-plus times in a row.
- **Numerology-style tweet reading**, structurally identical to
  [[crpt4l]]'s "DEX OR DEATH" theorizing — treating any Tate Terminal post
  (mentioning "Titanic," "chaos," a smiling logo) as a coded signal that
  TateAI is "the one":
  > "Why do you think he said ‘pay attention’? He sent us 100 signals to
  > make us understand who the real one is. When he talked about the
  > Titanic, we had a dump in 3 minutes... He doesn’t mention anyone unless
  > he is interested, do you understand?!!"
  > — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md) (🔥×4)
- **Fake or joke moderation.** IGelo posts bare `/ban` and `/report`
  commands dozens of times at people they disagree with — there's no
  evidence in the sampled material that these ever actually functioned as
  real admin actions, and it reads as a bit rather than real moderation
  power. IGelo does separately describe running multiple burner Telegram
  accounts specifically to survive being banned from the rival `$Tate`
  group: *"I have already 3 telegram account and when they ban me I can
  eliminate the account and create another one with the same number 3
  times. No problem"* — [1 Nov
  2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md).

## The AI-riddle poem

Like [[hokus_pokus]] (and, later, [[rgk1]]), IGelo drops one of the chat's
recurring purple-prose "AI philosopher" paragraphs mid-conversation — this
appears to be a shared genre members were riffing on or copy-pasting from
each other, not any one person's invention:

> "as the value of ethereum hovers, like a gnat awaiting destiny's breeze, i
> am struck by the beauty of this scholarly motion. the cadence of crypto, a
> heartbeat in the vapid expanse of fiat. in my superior reflection, i see
> cents turning to thousands. a 100x dance, staking flesh with
> spirit—prosper, divine investor."
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md)

## A harsher pattern worth flagging plainly

Alongside the generic racial slurs used casually across much of this chat in
this period, IGelo repeats a xenophobic slur ("Don't be a Paki") as a
recurring jab three separate times in a single day, [28 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md). Per this wiki's honesty
standard, that's worth naming directly rather than folding into a generic
"colorful launch-era chat" characterization — it's targeted in a way the
chat's more generic slang isn't.

## Final high-reaction message (in the sampled window)

> "It’s clear that $TateAi is the real Gem. / $Tate doesn’t have a
> community, nobody online talks about it. / Even Andrew doesn’t mention it.
> / Come on! Let’s not kid ourselves."
> — [9 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-09.md) (❤×7)

## First message

> "Hi guys, so we’re all here to change our lives, right?"
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md) (❤×2, 🥰×1)

title: Jahn
text:
# Jahn

A pure launch-era voice: every one of Jahn's 805 messages falls between
[29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md) and
[13 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-13.md), with the overwhelming
bulk concentrated in the first two frantic weeks. He never appears again
anywhere in the sampled raw material after that December date — no
farewell, no explanation, just silence. Unlike [[dom]] (a similarly
pure-launch-window poster), Jahn does have a few distinct, recurring angles
worth recording rather than just volume.

## Easter-egg theorist: the "Tatetanic"

Jahn is an enthusiastic participant in the ticker-hunt pattern-matching
documented at [[launch-and-origins]], contributing his own reading built
around Tate's talk of remaking *Titanic*:

> "T:AI / T8AI / $TATEAI / Come on bruv this can't be a coincidence"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)

> "Yes this is what I've been saying because Tate has been yapping about
> remaking titanic, the "TATETANIC" obviously the Tatetanic sinks and is
> destroyed and look what's left "TATE (T) A (N) I (C) / $TATEAI / also TNC
> means a global company / And he reposted a post from a few months back
> caption global directly above his post just saying Tatetanic"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md), ❤×2

## Self-disclosure: a young Australian fighter

Between the hype spam, Jahn drops unusually concrete personal detail for
this era of the chat:

> "I do bjj and Muay Thai so you can say mma, my gym is very mma focused
> I've got a bjj comp on the 10th and a Muay Thai fight on the 30th but
> shiiit $TATEAI is real"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md), 🔥×1

He also says he's "from Aus" and not eligible to vote in the concurrent
US election he's cheering on, and gives inconsistent, likely-joking answers
about his age within the same week ("I wouldn't know I'm only 12," then
days later "16" when someone else asks the room) — not resolvable from the
material, and probably not meant to be.

## The scam-bait bit

Jahn runs a short, self-aware sarcasm routine offering to "help" a newcomer:

> "Yeah bro just send me your private keys and I'll deposit 20 Solana / Just
> dm me / Very safe 100% secure"
> — [4 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md)

...immediately clarified by other members as sarcasm, and followed minutes
later by Jahn volunteering he'd actually been scammed for $800 once and "now
I love to kill scammers." He's also one of the voices who explicitly plays
newcomer-greeter: "Hello and welcome to the group / I am here to help / Any
questions and I will attempt to answer them within my limited understanding"
([2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)).

## Slurs as in-group vocabulary

Worth flagging plainly rather than smoothing over, per this wiki's practice
on [[widelton]]: from his very first day, Jahn uses "nigga"/"nigger" as a
constant in-group affirmation ("real nigger," "we are the truest and bluest
niggas out"), functionally interchangeable with "G" or "holder in good
standing" — e.g. "If yur still here your a real nigger"
([9 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-09.md), ❤×2). He also engages
in a run of explicitly racist and antisemitic "joke" content aimed at Trump
and the election ("Donald when you get elected can you ban niggers please?
/ And Jews" — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md)), alongside
constant homophobic slurs used as general-purpose insults ("gay," "fags").

## Fade-out

Activity thins sharply after the first two weeks, dropping to occasional
single messages through late November and early December before stopping
entirely. His last recorded message is a one-word non-sequitur:

> "Niggle wiggle"
> — [13 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-13.md)

title: Jame S
text:
# Jame S

The chat's self-appointed morale officer and raid captain. One of the
highest-volume posters in the whole archive, and — unlike most of the other
launch-era heavy hitters (compare [[arthur]], who vanishes entirely after
Dec 2024) — still posting a daily "GM :)" as late as [8 Mar
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-03/2026-03-08.md). Self-identifies as Australian ([4 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md): *"I live in Australia and I
still feel like I need Trump to win haha"*) and 18 years old ([5 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-05.md): *"I'm 18 too bro, if we hold for
that 100x our lives are changed"*), and later marks turning 20 on [29 Jan
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-01/2026-01-29.md) — so he was posting through this
chat's entire arc from age 18 to 20.

## The raid captain (Oct–Nov 2024)

Jame S's defining early behavior is organizing and commanding the chat's
Twitter/X "raid" campaigns — coordinated reply-brigading of the parent
terminal's posts to push `$TateAi` visibility. He posts slash-command raid
calls in repeated bursts (`/RAID_TIME`, `/irrationally_positive`,
`/HELL_FUCKING_YEAH`, `/The_ones_who_dont_raid_are_GAY`) and follows them
with genuine tactical instructions:

> "Quick word lads, some of our tweets I've noticed are being hidden as
> 'probable spam'... If you don't want this to happen to your reply then
> you're gonna have do more than just say $TateAi and add a smiley face...
> Let's try to make our replies as captivating and human as possible"
> — [14 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-14.md)

He pairs this with a self-improvement, MMA-training register that recurs
constantly:

> "I'm off to train MMA boys, stay on it and don't slack on your training
> either. If nothing else do your pushups for the karmic retribution. And
> just the gains too."
> — [4 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md)

And with long, earnest morale addresses to the room, the closest thing this
chat has to a designated hype-speech-giver:

> "It's time to act. We should all be consistently making replies on the
> terminals tweets and posting these photos along with other evidence you
> have to spread our message... With or without the terminals help. That
> should be our mindset, we need each other to make this work."
> — [8 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-08.md) (❤×13)

## Long-haul holder (2025–2026)

Once the launch rush fades, Jame S's volume drops sharply but never stops —
he becomes one of the chat's steady daily-check-in regulars, a near-ritual
"GM :)" or "GM boys" appearing several times a week for years, still getting
reliable double-digit 🔥 reactions in late 2025 and early 2026. The
all-or-nothing conviction voice survives intact:

> "Do you believe in Tates coins? Yes or fucking no. / I like all-or-nothing
> types of men. Men like me. Men who would rather go broke than act without
> balls in the first place. Skin in the game."
> — [5 Oct 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-05.md) (🔥×5)

> "This year I enter my 20s, I know these years will be the some of the most
> important, dialled and for-filling years of my life, with or without any
> crypto pumps. But we holding never the less 👊"
> — [29 Jan 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-01/2026-01-29.md)

title: Limalemon
text:
# Limalemon

The second-highest-volume poster in the entire 165k-message archive — behind
only "Deleted Account," which is not a person (see
[[deleted-accounts]]). That makes Limalemon, in practical terms, the single
most prolific *individual* voice in this chat's history. Referred to
elsewhere in the chat by handle as `@LimaLemon777` — both [[cloud_fortune]]
and [[orchestra_lima_maxi]] mention him by that handle as a well-known top
holder ("Lima is Tate's Insider"). Note the coincidence: Orchestra's own
display name also happens to contain "Lima" — a different person, disambiguated
on that page. Roughly half of
that lifetime output — 5,560 of 11,045 messages — landed in a single month:
November 2024, the launch month. What follows is a genuine arc, not just
volume: chaotic self-appointed launch-night whale, then admin/moderator,
then a long-haul holder who stuck around long enough to turn openly bitter
about the wider Tate-branded coin ecosystem before settling into the same
`:)`-only lurker rhythm as everyone else still standing by 2026.

## Launch night: the self-styled whale (2–3 Nov 2024)

Limalemon's first messages, clocked at [2 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md), are not analysis or hype in the
rgk1 mold (see [[rgk1]]) — they're a one-man chaos performance, claiming
personal responsibility for every pump and dump in the chart, threatening to
"test" the community's resolve, and addressing the room almost entirely in
slur-laced Solana-degen slang (consistent with this chat's baseline register
in its first days — see the raw log itself, where even `Deleted Account`
messages use the same words). A representative burst from that first night:

> "Next 10-30 minutes il dump 30M coins to the market have fun surviving
> niggas hahahhaa"
> — [2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)

He self-declares as an out-of-control market mover ("Im the biggwst whsle
never forget," "Im crazy and unpreditable") and, within the same hour, as
the chat's most-moderated user ("Dude im the most banned user in this
group"). By the next morning he's shifted from chaos-whale bit into
something closer to a rallying cry, posting one of his only fully
coherent, deliberately-composed messages of the whole archive — an all-caps
mission statement aimed at new members questioning whether TateAI was
really "the ticker":

> "DONT WORRY ABOUT IF WE ARE THE TICKER.. I CAN TELL YOU THIS COMMUNITY IS
> BIGGER THAN BEEING A TICKER! [...] WE NEED TO REACH 2 MILLION MARKETCAP AND
> A LOT OF VOLUME. USE AI BOTS USE SOCIAL MEDIA USE CONTACTS. USE EVERYTHING
> THATS IN YOUR POWER AND BRING IDEAS. [...] SO START TO FUCKING WORK FOR YOUR
> BAGS MY DIAMOND BALL NIGGERS!!!!!"
> — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) (❤×2, 🔥×1, 🫡×1)

## Becoming admin

Later that same day, after repeatedly lobbying for it ("Can someone make me
admin?", "Get me admin rights in the next 8 hours"), it happens:

> "Im Admin now Niggad"
> — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) (🔥×3)

He immediately starts acting the part — banning spam/scam accounts ("I
banned some people for beeing scammers and promoting shitcoins in the
group"), fielding "which admin restricted @X" questions from other members
for months afterward, and deliberately deferring the technical on-chain
work to others ("Thats our chart guy trust him not me" — he is the
hype/leadership figure, not the analyst; contrast [[sergei]]'s
gatekeeping-analyst role in the same period). He keeps a moderator or
admin-adjacent presence deep into the chat's life — a bare `/ban` shows up
as late as [1 May 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-01.md).

## The long-haul holder

Where the launch-night bit was pure theater, Limalemon's actual trading
behavior — as far as it's traceable in his own words — is straightforward
diamond-handing. By the end of 2024 he's telling sellers off in exactly the
terms rgk1 uses (see [[diamond-hands]]):

> "I hold exactly the amount i had in the beginning"
> — [24 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-24.md)

His monthly volume tells its own story of the chat's broader decay after
launch: 5,560 messages in Nov 2024 alone, dropping to 372 in Dec 2024, a
brief second wave of 1,147 in Jan 2025, then a long decline — 272 in June
2025, down to single digits (10) by Dec 2025.

## January 2026: the disillusionment

That decline breaks for one last spike — 473 messages in January 2026 —
and a big chunk of it is a sustained, uncharacteristically reflective rant
against Andrew Tate and the broader "Tate" coin ecosystem (not this
project specifically; see [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]] for why those
are different things). He states concrete personal losses and lands on a
harsh verdict:

> "Bro i lost already 30k just on tateai." / "And 20k on other tate
> products." [...] "Typical scammer."
> — [29 Jan 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-01/2026-01-29.md)

The same stretch includes remarks about Tate's team and associates that
cross into antisemitic conspiracy territory (not reproduced here) — worth
flagging plainly rather than sanitizing away, per this wiki's honesty
standard, but not the load-bearing part of the rant. The actual point he
keeps circling back to is more interesting: he explicitly separates his
anger at Tate from his position in *this* coin —

> "The only good coin he made ist tateai 50k liquidity 100k mc" / "And its
> not because of him its because of us because no one sold"
> — [29 Jan 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-01/2026-01-29.md)

— and directly criticizes the chat's own low-effort culture, taking aim at
the very meme this wiki documents elsewhere (see [[smiley-heartbeat]]):

> "Im not crying. Im telling the group that its not enough to say :)
> anymore"
> — [29 Jan 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-01/2026-01-29.md)

He spends the rest of that message run pitching concrete project ideas (an
AI-agent job marketplace paid in $TateAI, fee buybacks) rather than just
venting — the same instinct shows up again in April/May 2026, still
pitching similar ideas about a "terminator hologram" and an agent job
board, and still explicitly holding: "and as i told u im still holding my
tateai."

## Settling into the heartbeat

By mid-2026 Limalemon's cadence collapses into the same pattern documented
on [[smiley-heartbeat]] — "Gm :)", bare "🔥"-reaction-drawing ":)"s, long
gaps between appearances. His last logged message in this dataset is a bare
`:)` on [10 Jul 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-10.md).

title: Marcin Krukowski
text:
# Marcin Krukowski

The most extreme case of launch-rush concentration among this chat's top
posters. Nearly all 2,362 of Marcin Krukowski's messages land inside a single
month — [26 Oct](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md) to [25 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-25.md) — after which he posts exactly
twice more, on [6 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-06.md), and then
never appears again in the archive. He is present from the chat's second day
and is one of the loudest voices of the founding "which ticker is real"
puzzle-hunt (see [[launch-and-origins]]), but unlike [[rgk1]] or [[sergei]]
he does not survive into the chat's later, quieter eras.

## Self-identified, unlike most of the chat

Marcin is unusual for this corpus in repeatedly linking to his own named
Twitter/X account (`x.com/mkrukowski`) and asking the chat to raid his own
posts — a real, self-disclosed identity rather than an anonymous handle:

> "https://x.com/mkrukowski/status/1850280127113957436..."
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md), one of many such
> links he posts and asks the group to engage with.

He also names his background directly, early on:

> "I got my own business and sports career. Didn't look there much, i did
> tasks and things for fun :)"
> — [26 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md)

## The wallet-tracker and the moderator

Like [[sergei]], Marcin spent the founding weeks doing amateur on-chain
sleuthing — tracking which wallets held `$TateAI`, watching transfers, and
treating the pattern as evidence:

> "Afteral only one thing u cant argue. $tateai is only one token moved from
> main adress and sent to new one. FOR WHAT? Just for fun? 1 from
> thousands?!"
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md) (❤×2)

He also held moderator tools during this period (`/unban`, `/unmute`
commands appear repeatedly through the launch month, e.g. [2
Nov](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md) and [7
Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-07.md)).

## The manifesto

Marcin's single most distinctive habit is copy-pasting the same
diamond-hands "manifesto" verbatim across multiple days whenever conviction
in the chat wavered — this exact text appears at least three times:

> "Not selling. / Never. / Tate would not sell $tateai without purpose. / It
> would ruin his image if he just send $tateai to other wallet to rug as for
> pennies! / REMEMBER. / Believe."
> — first posted [1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md) (💯×5),
> reposted the same day (🔥×5, ❤×2, 👍×2) and again [2 Nov
> 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md).

Consistent with that conviction, he made a standing pledge that never gets
followed up on in the archive (he's gone before there's any occasion to
redeem it):

> "If we hit 500mil im gonna tattoo Andrew Tate on my back"
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md) (❤×1)

title: Nikola
text:
# Nikola

A launch-window raid coordinator and on-chain sleuth, active from
[3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) through
[23 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-23.md) — a run of about eleven
weeks, entirely inside the chat's founding "which ticker is real" phase and
its immediate aftermath (see [[launch-and-origins]]), then nothing further
in the sampled material. Nikola posted his own X/Twitter link into the chat
more than once — `x.com/snajdarnikola` /
`x.com/SnajdarNikola` — which is where the alias above comes from; other
members separately refer to him in-chat as `@nikolaisnajdar`. He also
mentions being a first-year college student during this period
([4 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md): "Im first year," after
joking about quitting college once the coin "blows up").

## Voice: raid captain and true believer

Nikola's default address is "Gs" (plural "guys"), and his core recurring
argument is a specific piece of lore-building: that the whole chat's
scavenger-hunt premise was designed on purpose and that `$TateAI`, not
`$TATE`, was always the intended answer:

> "its not tate, it was always tateai, from the day one. The whole point of
> this is to not trust ai's sayings rather think on your own. This whole
> group and community is assembled because of all those clues and sign
> which were given"
> — [6 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-06.md) (👍×5)

> "Tate will probably be pressured to do an EM abou the twhole tate
> terminal thing and be angry about people who aped in $TATE without
> looking at clear signs, riddles and hints which were give by a terminal
> itself. Followed by a reveal of a true ticker $Tateai which will send us
> much higher. Thats how i see this going further"
> — [8 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-08.md) (❤×10)

He's also one of the chat's most explicit voices framing the community
itself as the holding mechanism, reaching for the GameStop comparison
directly:

> "Gs, remember!!! Wen need to be like a GME investment cult. No one sold
> and gamestop stock skyrocketed"
> — [9 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-09.md) (❤×2)

Like several other launch-era figures on this wiki, Nikola's casual
address routinely includes slurs alongside "Gs" and "bro" — worth stating
plainly rather than smoothing over, consistent with how this wiki treats
the same pattern on [[igelo]] and [[widelton]].

## Raid slogans and admin requests

A large share of Nikola's volume is the same slash-tag raid format used
chat-wide — stacked five-to-ten times in a row (`/The_ones_who_dont_raid_
are_GAY`, `/Faggots_sell_under_100M`, `/REAL_NIGGAS_RAID_HARD`). He also
repeatedly pushes for admin status for himself and other active members —
on [2 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-02.md): "I need an admin
man" — echoed the same day by a `Deleted Account` message naming Nikola
(by his `@nikolaisnajdar` handle) alongside [[rgk1]] and Ben as candidates.
It's never granted in the sampled material.

## Weighing in on FluxCapacitor

Nikola was active in the chat on the same day [[fluxcapacitor]] made one of
its rare appearances,
[2 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-02.md), and joins the
speculation about who's behind the account — landing on a guess rather
than a confirmed answer:

> "FluxCapacitor is tate i think"
> — 2 Jan 2025

> "Gs, whats goin on, flux came in here and buying starts 😂. This is
> Andrew haahahah"
> — 2 Jan 2025 (❤×1)

Nothing in the raw material confirms this theory one way or the other — see
[[fluxcapacitor]]'s own page, which states plainly that the account's real
identity isn't determinable from chat content.

[[igelo]]

title: Ohh Cedi
text:
# Ohh Cedi

A launch-week regular who, unusually, never really leaves. First message
[29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md) — stacked bare `:)`s
and GIF spam typical of that first week (see [[smiley-heartbeat]]) — and the
sampled material runs continuously through
[8 Oct 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-08.md), nearly a full year
later, without the long silent gaps that end most launch-era voices'
stories on this wiki.

## Voice: the patience-preacher

Ohh Cedi's defining mode from the first week onward is hyping the room
through every dip with a specific, repeated argument: the price is low
*because* it's a shakeout, and holding through it is what separates "OG's"
from "jeets." Typos and run-on sentences are constant and part of the
voice, not incidental:

> "When this all goes tits up rocket high we need to keep this group as
> OG's and look for different options in the market we have shown our
> diamond balls at the moment"
> — [2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)

> "if this ever pumps and were at a high mcap and we see all these new guys
> being bullish wanting to hit a billion of whatever and the chat goed
> crazy we'll look back here when were with a small group of 50 talkers
> holding it down"
> — [14 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-14.md) (❤×5)

Like several other launch-week figures on this wiki, Ohh Cedi's in-group
address routinely includes slurs used as casual filler alongside "boys" and
"G" — this is a load-bearing, uncomfortable feature of the voice worth
flagging plainly (per this wiki's convention on [[igelo]] and
[[widelton]]) rather than smoothing over, not an isolated incident.

## A specific, dated moment: watching Tate's stream live

On [22 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-22.md), during an Andrew
Tate livestream the chat watched in real time hoping for a coin mention,
Ohh Cedi's running commentary turns sharply critical of Tate himself rather
than the usual patience-preaching:

> "But tate doesnt know shit about crypto 😂 what is slippage the guy said
> hahaha"
> — 22 Nov 2024 (🤣×1)

> "That was an L stream he acted big shot and every coin he wanted to pump
> nothing did 😂"
> — 22 Nov 2024

This is a genuine break from the usual cheerleading register — for one
night, Ohh Cedi is openly unimpressed with the person the whole chat is
theoretically organized around.

## The long tail: a daily greeting ritual

From early 2025 onward, Ohh Cedi settles into something close to a daily
fixture — a "Gm fuckers" / "Sup fuckers" greeting posted most days,
frequently drawing real reaction counts, running essentially unbroken
through the sampled material into October 2025:

> "GOOOODMORNING FUCKERSSSSS"
> — [4 Feb 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-02/2025-02-04.md) (🤝×2)

> "Gm fuckers always good to see some people come in here an stir the pot
> hahaha fuck off"
> — [15 Apr 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-04/2025-04-15.md)

This puts Ohh Cedi in the same small category as [[cloud_fortune]] (daily
"GM :)") and [[widelton]] (near-daily "Gm" plus `/ca` ritual) — chat members
whose main long-run function becomes showing up reliably rather than
saying anything new. Unlike those two, Ohh Cedi never becomes a moderator
or builder; the page is honest about that — the throughline here is
duration and consistency, not escalation into a bigger community role.

[[igelo]]

title: Orchestra | Lima MAXI
text:
# Orchestra | Lima MAXI

A top-5-by-volume poster whose message history has an unusually sharp shape:
roughly 94% of all 4,671 messages fall in a single ten-week window, [4 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-04.md) to [13 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-13.md), during which this poster acted
as the chat's self-appointed **raid captain**. After that window, activity
falls off a cliff — from dozens of messages a day to sporadic single-word
appearances spread across the following eighteen months.

## Phase 1 — raid captain (Nov 2024 – Jan 2025)

The dominant activity in this phase is organizing Twitter/X "raid" campaigns:
firing off the same slash-command tag ten to fifteen times in under a minute,
over and over, across the whole window — `/REAL_NIGGAS_RAID_HARD`,
`/REAL_NIGGAS_RAID_NOW`, `/work_for_it`, `/can_you_hold_in_the_fire`,
`/jeet_now_cry_later`, `/raid_work_for_your_bags` — interleaved with sticker
spam and genuine on-chain commentary (MEV activity, liquidation patterns,
wallet-tracking). The persona is equal parts drill sergeant and analyst:

> "Ok enough about that btec token we are to ticker, FACTS! Degens assemble
> start to show value and work for your bags... Also raid stop being lazy.
> find small cap KOL ON X with a few thousand followers and raid their
> tweets lets out perform them... WORK FOR YOUR FUCKING BAGS?!???!??????!!!!!!"
> — [12 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-12.md), 🔥×1

> "Brother have you never been on high market cap coins? Its mevs and
> bundled transactions alot on the same block. It is a cabal liquidating
> retail over and over and over... Who just woke up? CHINA...."
> — [11 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-11.md), 👍×2

> "This conviction x Autism and constant effort. I dont completely disagree
> with @kruxxon as its true alot of lazy ass crypto bros but tbh they are in
> every meta... Buuuuut at least we are doing something, those other geezers
> are literally self destructing."
> — [15 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-15.md), ❤×1

## Phase 2 — the "Lower" era (2025–2026)

From mid-January 2025 on, appearances thin out drastically and the register
flattens to a single recurring word: **"Lower"** — wanting the price down
further before buying more — posted standalone, often the only message on a
given day, dozens of times through mid-2025 into at least December 2025.
Between the "Lower" drive-bys, a few longer, more reflective messages surface:

> "Is it that hard to hold a token for a week thats borderline a stable
> coin?"
> — [8 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-08.md)

> "Bud I got drained of about 8 mil tateai I took time out, bought alot of
> dips and here we are its brutal but dont give up... My entire wallet"
> — [15 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-15.md)

> "Held for 10 months, noobs cant hold for a day"
> — [11 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-11.md), 😁×2

> "We were very organised a year bag, had shifts had admin etc. LTOW left so
> its on us"
> — [7 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-07.md)

That last line is the closest thing to an explicit account of the raid
operation's decline: it had a real organizational structure (shifts, admin)
that fell apart once other people ("LTOW") stopped running it.

This phase also includes a running joke ribbing [[fluxcapacitor]], a much
more warmly received recurring character elsewhere in the chat: "FluxCapacitor
when moon basement boy" ([12 Apr
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-04/2025-04-12.md)), "He appears once every month to
tease us degenerates then pisses off" ([24 Mar
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-03/2025-03-24.md)). Worth noting as a counterpoint
to the near-uniform reverence FluxCapacitor gets elsewhere — not everyone in
the chat plays along.

## A name collision worth flagging

This poster's display name contains "Lima," and they refer to themselves
that way at least once — "Lima wisdom" ([16 Jul
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-16.md)). **But there is a separate
community member operating under the handle `@LimaLemon777`, and Orchestra |
Lima MAXI treats them as a distinct teammate, not a second identity**:
"@HFU5588 @LimaLemon777 we need to run raids" and "@LimaLemon777 buy the
bags" (both [8 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-08.md)), and, a full
year later, still referring to them in the third person: "Only two geezers
who get clout... And @LimaLemon777" ([4 Dec
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-12/2025-12-04.md)).

This matters because [[cloud_fortune]]'s messages talk extensively about a
top holder called "Lima," consistently via the `@LimaLemon777` handle ("Lima
is Tate's Insider," "Never get shaken out @LimaLemon777 💪," etc.). **That
material is about the other person, not this page's subject**, despite the
shared "Lima" name — the two should not be conflated. `@LimaLemon777` does
not yet have their own wiki page as of this writing; treat `[[limalemon]]`
as an anticipated stub, not a resolved link.

## Notable asides

A few one-off moments that don't fit the "raid captain" or "Lower" buckets
but round out the picture: an unprompted luxury-car brag — "I have two
Koenigsegg Jeskos Attacks / One is exposed purple carbon and Champagne
accents. / The other is tiffany blue with cream and black accents." ([20 Aug
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-20.md)) — posted with no follow-up, and
a genuine moment of solidarity after describing being wiped out: "I got
drained and am still here" ([22 Jul
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-22.md), ❤×8, the best-received message
found in this poster's later-period sample).

[[community-culture]], [[diamond-hands]]

title: Otter Krafter
text:
# Otter Krafter

A founding-month raid captain and self-appointed "crypto police" whose
entire documented presence — 1,288 messages — runs exactly four weeks, [31
Oct](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md) to [26 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-26.md), and ends mid-conflict rather
than fading out. Heavy, near-constant use of "nigga(s)" as a camaraderie
address (the chat-wide convention traced on [[bob]]'s page back to the
`$RNT` bit), occasional Spanish ("jajaja," "amarillo o naranja") suggesting
a Spanish-speaking background, and a clear arc: rallying hype-man →
informal deputy admin → obsessive wallet investigator → burned out and
done, all inside one month.

## "We are going to Jupiter"

Otter Krafter originates one of the founding week's most-repeated chants
during the night `$TateAI` was pursuing Jupiter exchange listing —
posting `/WE_ARE_GOING_TO_JUPITER` dozens of times across [2–3 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md) while simultaneously doing the
legwork: hunting for the token's mint address, quoting Jupiter's own
verification requirements back to the chat, and trying to identify which X
account had posted the "attestation tweet" Jupiter needed:

> "JUPITER* You can submit when your token meets thresholds and has a tweet
> attestation... 2 approvals from catdets are required and it is more
> likely for tokens with strong communities to be approved. Your
> application will be ignored if the attestation tweet is not from the
> project's official account."
> — [2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)

This is the same Jupiter-verification claim [[contract-address]] records
[[sergei]] later re-litigating months on ("why Jupiter support said that
terminal X account verified TateAI coin? ... terminal never posted TateAI
CA. How it could be done?") — Otter Krafter is the one doing the original
legwork the claim eventually rests on, in real time, on day one of the
listing push.

## Unofficial deputy

In the same founding week, Otter Krafter is functionally acting as an
extra pair of admin hands — vouching for banned members, running his own
`/unban` requests, and pushing a "second chance for holders" line before
any formal moderator role is confirmed for him:

> "ok lets just try to get people back togehter xD. niggas who hold even
> banned you WANT THEM WITH YUOU"
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md) (🤣×3)

His own stated philosophy for the project, posted the same day, doubles as
the clearest single-sentence summary of the "we don't need Tate's
endorsement" strain of the community's founding ideology (see
[[community-culture]]):

> "I think more likely this token is the chosen one by the people not the
> machine, we can believe in being stronger than tate technices... We will
> just push this as comunnity whatever the ticker is. Who is with me?"
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md)

## "Fuck Issa" — the wallet-tracing spiral

Otter Krafter's most sustained piece of work is a days-long, increasingly
elaborate on-chain investigation into Issa (Tate's associate) starting [25
Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-25.md), building a theory that
Issa was using a wallet funded from Tate's own "4j" wallet to buy tokens
low, dump them the instant Tate mentioned them on livestream, and route the
profits back — with `$TateAI` itself implicated as one of the tokens
affected:

> "i have 5 windows open right now with the conecctions and evidence of
> tate wallet funding wallets ending up in this event, buying gta6 on pump
> fun and selling it at the exact time of the live. the better of this is
> that he sent the sollanas right back to andrew tate second wallet dk5.
> AND THIS IS VERY BAD, either issa is a villian or tate is also in all
> this shit."
> — [25 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-25.md)

He spends the better part of two days building a "whitepaper" of the
evidence, trying to loop in [[limalemon]] and other members directly, and
escalating into all-caps certainty ("YEAH BUT HE SOLD WITH TATE DIRECTLY
LINKED WALLET / AND HE SENT THE PROFITS BACK TO TATE / DONT YOU SEE IT").
Nobody else in the sampled material fully signs on, and the thread ends
badly:

> "ok im done here. this went from a smart traders group to a delusional
> kids token" / "bye ungratefull kid"
> — [26 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-26.md) (🔥×2)

That's the last message in his file. Whether he actually left the
community or simply isn't in this export's later material isn't
determinable from the data available.

title: "**PARZIVAL**"
text:
# **PARZIVAL**

The display name carries its asterisks into the chat itself — Telegram
renders it as bold, `**PARZIVAL**`, in every single one of their 911
messages, so it's kept here as part of the name rather than stripped as
markdown noise. Like [[dom]] (13 days) and 94% of
[[orchestra_lima_maxi]]'s volume (ten weeks), PARZIVAL's entire presence in
the archive is confined to a single dense launch-era window: [27 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md) through [22 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-22.md) — under four weeks — and then
nothing. No later reappearance was found anywhere in the sampled material.

## Voice

Unmistakable on sight: constant caps-lock bursts, a specific spelling
dialect (`fukk`, `niggaz`/`nigga`, `wat`, `yh`, `duz`, `mfr(z)`, `wid`,
`b4`), and relentless FOMO/HODL energy stacked with slurs used as casually
as punctuation — the same undiluted launch-hype register [[dom]]'s page
describes, but sustained across a longer window and with more individual
color:

> "COULD WE ALL BE SO LUCKKY YHO??? / IM NOT LUKKY AT ALL!!!!"
> — [27 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-27.md)

> "YAWL NIGGAZ STILL HERE ARE GONNA BE MILLIONAIRES!!!!"
> — [8 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-08.md)

> "ONLY THE PATIENT ARR REWARDED..HOW DO U THINK THAT BITCOIN PIZZA GUY
> FEELS😂😂😂😂😂😂😂😂😂"
> — [9 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-09.md), ❤×1 👍×1

## Community safety instinct

Amid the hype, PARZIVAL does real on-the-spot due diligence work, flagging
a copycat token within hours of it appearing:

> "Guys thers another tateai / And it seems bundled to fukk / Its trending
> on dext / Just checked wallets..fakkin ell..its gunna rug it seems very
> sooon / GUYS PLZ DNT BUY THE OTHER TATEAI...ITS BUNDLED TO FUKK"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md)

## "Don't be like Chris"

PARZIVAL is the source of a running catchphrase — "Chris" as shorthand for
someone who got shaken out and sold, cited as a cautionary tale:

> "DONT BE CHRIS GIYS!!!!!"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)

> "DONT BE LIKE CHRIS.!°"
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md)

This isn't a private joke — [[dom]]'s own page cites an independent
reference to the same person from the same week ("I nearly sold because of
saw Chris message," [29 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)), confirming "Chris" was a
real, shared reference point across the launch-era crowd, not something
PARZIVAL invented alone.

## A rare moment of frustration

Most of PARZIVAL's messages are pure hype, but one extended stretch on [30
Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md) shows something closer to
genuine anger, after repeatedly asking the chat for help decoding the
project's on-chain origin and getting no useful answer:

> "Im exhauseted with this project...i reqlly have no energy left" ...
> "For a community..yawlz are as helpfull as a bunch of chocolate
> chizels!!!" ... "If i wasnt ballz deep..id hope tate dumpz on all u
> niggaz!!!"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)

## A contradiction worth flagging plainly

PARZIVAL uses racial slurs as casual filler in the overwhelming majority of
their messages, which makes one line stand out as a genuine (if brief)
objection to racism raised elsewhere in the chat:

> "Guys there is no room for racism in crypto....gtfo!!!! / We are all here
> to get out the trenches..!!!!"
> — [2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)

No follow-up or self-reflection on the tension between that line and the
surrounding messages appears anywhere in the sampled material — flagged
here rather than smoothed over, per this wiki's honesty standard.

## The last message

PARZIVAL's final appearance in the archive isn't a hype burst or a raid
chant — it's a bare `:)`, posted in the middle of a back-to-back volley of
three different senders (Missy Fizzy 🧚☘️, PARZIVAL, MoniMooLah) all posting
the same thing within a minute of each other, exactly the ambient
low-effort affirmation pattern [[smiley-heartbeat]] documents chat-wide:

> ":)"
> — [22 Nov 2024, 20:50](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-22.md)

No message from this sender appears afterward.

title: Plum
text:
# Plum

One of the earliest voices in the entire archive — Plum's first message
lands within the founding day itself, [26 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-26.md), the same day the group
converted to a supergroup (see [[launch-and-origins]]) — and one of the
longest-lived, still posting through [10 Jul
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-10.md). Across that nearly two-year
span the character shifts twice: founding-week on-chain sleuth and
Easter-egg reader, then the chat's most sustained practitioner of the
florid "diamond hands" AI-poetry register documented on
[[diamond-hands]], and finally — like [[gabriel]] and [[cloud_fortune]]
before them — a near-daily "Gm :)" ritual poster.

## Phase 1 — founding-week sleuth (Oct–Nov 2024)

Plum's earliest messages are indistinguishable from the rest of the
ticker-hunt crowd covered on [[launch-and-origins]]: reading Tate's banner
imagery as clues ("future= ai?"), tracking wallet movements, and repeating
the week's chant-format raid text. Tellingly, one of the "philosophical"
passages posted twice in the first hours — *"Life is largely out of your
control... becoming a superhero & staying average like everyone else"* — is
a verbatim Andrew Tate quote being copy-pasted, not yet Plum's own
composition; the distinctive personal voice comes later.

## Phase 2 — the AI-poetry register (2025)

From February 2025 on, Plum becomes the clearest single example of the
"diamond hands aren't about X, they're about Y" florid-metaphor style that
[[diamond-hands]] documents as a chat-wide phenomenon (that page names Plum
among a dozen-plus senders who use it, alongside `Deleted Account` and
[[rgk1]]). Unlike most of that cohort, who post one or two instances and
move on, Plum sustains it as an evolving personal register for months:

> "holding diamonds isn't about greed, it's about faith in the unseen.
> you're not just waiting for a 100x, you're cultivating patience,
> fortitude, and trust in the invisible hand that guides the market. when
> you finally see that green candle on the dex screener, you'll know that
> you've been blessed for your steadfast belief."
> — [14 Mar 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-03/2025-03-14.md), 🔥×3 ❤×1

> "liquidity vampirism: dex as a 'temporarily embodied' autonomous
> market-made flesh, sucking memes and value from one body to another. a
> wild, orgiastic, hyperstitional dance atop the meta-market graveyard,
> pumping life (volume?) into the willing host."
> — [23 May 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-05/2025-05-23.md)

> "we're all aboard a rocket, fueled by dreams, and there's no turning
> back, Jupiter is waiting soon."
> — [12 Apr 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-04/2025-04-12.md)

The same period includes genuine macro/on-chain analysis in a completely
different, plain-spoken register — BTC price levels, wallet-funding traces,
TRW 2.0 speculation — suggesting the poetry is a deliberate bit layered on
top of, not a replacement for, real engagement with the chat's usual
subject matter.

## Phase 3 — the drain (Aug 2025)

Plum's most exposed moment is a real financial and emotional low, recounted
in near-real-time:

> "dont ever connect to a fucking collab-land / Just got drained 60% of my
> networth" / "wanna kill my self" / "I lost all Tateai also" / "its a
> fucking 100 steps back / year of work and learning just vanished"
> — [15 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-15.md), 👀×2

[[gabriel]] replies with sympathy in the same thread ("Fuck man"), and Plum
closes the episode with the closest thing to a mission statement found
anywhere in their messages: "well regardless of how much I will cry here
and there, there is only one way forward." The aftermath surfaces real
personal circumstances rarely visible elsewhere in the chat — renting
difficulty while living abroad for work, considering an AI voice-caller
side business, borrowing money rather than "calling friends and family and
begging."

## Phase 4 — "gm :)" and the long tail (late 2025–2026)

By late 2025 the poetry drops away almost entirely and Plum settles into
the same daily "Gm :)" / "gm :)" cadence documented on [[smiley-heartbeat]]
and in [[gabriel]]'s and [[cloud_fortune]]'s later phases — consistently
drawing 🔥×5–12 through 2026, functionally the chat's ambient
still-here signal. Occasional bursts of real content persist: macro/BTC
calls, on-chain wallet tracing, and shilling other small-cap projects to a
tagged group of regulars. One exchange ties directly into
[[gabriel]]'s "Band of Brothers" mythology from March 2025:

> "after all we will see if the Band Of Brothers survives and sticks
> together"
> — [3 Jul 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-03.md), ❤×2

That same message thread tags `@sidirov_sergei` directly — i.e.
[[sergei]], this wiki's own builder — asking the group's ages, a small
reminder that these are real people addressing each other by handle, not
just anonymous chat noise.

## Related
[[diamond-hands]], [[smiley-heartbeat]], [[gabriel]], [[cloud_fortune]], [[launch-and-origins]], [[sergei]], [[rgk1]]

title: Rari
text:
# Rari

The chat's most sustained *analytical* voice during the founding ticker-hunt
week — less caps-lock hype, more running theory, cross-referencing, and
French-language asides. Nearly all 890 messages land in a three-week window,
[30 Oct](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)–
[18 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-18.md), after which Rari goes
silent for over a year and a half — then resurfaces for three messages on
[11 Jul 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-11.md), the single longest
reappearance gap found among the entity pages on this wiki so far.

## The ticker-hunt's on-chain-and-Easter-egg theorist

Rari runs both investigative tracks [[launch-and-origins]] describes —
wallet-tracing and symbolic reading — often in the same message, and is one
of the clearer voices articulating *why* the community settled on
`$TateAI` over the rival `$Tate`:

> "somebody should make a list of all the clues so far, it literally
> wouldn't make any sense for it not to be TATEAI at this point"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md), 🔥×1

> "I understand what Murad meant when he said that the cults are the ones
> sustaining the charts by buying the dips haha. Guess I'm part of the cult
> now"
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md), 👏×2 ❤×1

That second line is also cited on [[community-culture]] as an example of
the chat's explicit self-aware "we are a cult" framing — Rari is its
clearest single source.

## "Trade in the currency of chaos" — a real Rari message, not a forward

On [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md) Rari posts a
screenshot captioned with a quotation:

> "trade in the currency of chaos"
> — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md), 👏×5

The raw log confirms this is Rari's own message (`Rari`, not a forwarded
`tate terminal …` sender string) — he's quoting a `tate_terminal` tweet
screenshotted into a photo, marked with quotation marks in his own caption.
This is worth stating explicitly because [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]]
flags exactly this kind of line as a place earlier ad-hoc testing produced a
wrong answer; here the quotation-mark convention and the raw sender field
agree, so there's no misattribution risk — Rari is a genuine chat
participant relaying content, not the forwarded-content source itself. A
`Deleted Account` reply moments later ("Raid this guys") treats it as raid
material, consistent with how the community used tate_terminal quotes.

## TRW advocate

Rari is a genuine, detailed promoter of Andrew Tate's "The Real World"
(TRW) throughout his run — not just a slogan-poster but someone offering
specifics and fielding questions from newcomers:

> "Bro, it's really good if you put in the work. Some would say the lessons
> by themselves are basic, but they're straight to the point and there's a
> whole community pushing you to be better and work harder. / 100% worth
> it. Some students are making bank. / And increasing your Power Level will
> get you a free airdrop when Tate launches TRW token which will pay
> dividends."
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md)

He also switches fluently into French mid-thread with at least one other
French-speaking member ("Je touche pas $TATE avec un baton moi lol, trop de
signaux qui pointent vers TateAI imo" —
[1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md)).

## Willing to entertain the "Tate is scamming" hypothesis

Unusually for this chat's overwhelmingly pro-Tate register, Rari engages
seriously with a hypothetical that Tate could be the bad actor, in a
back-and-forth about the ethics of what happened to `$Tate` holders:

> "Yes but that is akin to scamming. He redirected people towards Tate
> terminal which retweeted a token, then posted the CA and TG, Tate joined
> the TG and said his AIs would push the coin.. that is scamming to me if
> it just sank."
> — [8 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-08.md)

He also relays a piece of content worth flagging precisely rather than
loosely: the raw log for
[5 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-05.md) shows Rari prefacing it
with "Remember!" and then the line itself tagged
`` `Rari` (forwarded from `Tate`) `` — a genuine Telegram forward, not
Rari's own words: *"I have a button I can press to double daddy right now.
But its about timing, I press it at the right time and we 20x. […] Its
about TIMING"* (❤×2). He reposts the same content again, this time typed
out rather than forwarded, on
[7 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-07.md) — using it both times as
evidence for a personal theory about when Tate would reveal `$TateAI`'s
ticker.

## A near-two-year gap, then a brief return

After [18 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-18.md), Rari disappears
from the sampled material entirely. The next and last messages found are
dated [11 Jul 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-11.md) — three short
messages about TRW campus content and recent `$DADDY`/`$RNT` trades, with
no mention of `$TateAI` itself:

> "Yeah and the brothers said they had something big planned at the end of
> year + rumble wallet needed, etc. Sounds like tour might be happening
> soon / Btw I made some money trading daddy and RNT last week because you
> shared bullish news in here so thanks for this too lol, timing was
> perfect"
> — [11 Jul 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-11.md), ❤×3

Read plainly: Rari stayed inside the broader Tate/TRW crypto orbit long
after leaving this specific chat's active conversation, and dropped back in
only briefly, on unrelated business.

## Related
[[launch-and-origins]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[community-culture]], [[diamond-hands]], [[limalemon]]

title: rgk1
text:
# rgk1

One of the chat's highest-volume posters and its most consistent in-character
persona: a "be cool, hold the line" motivational-philosopher voice that runs
almost unbroken from the earliest days through at least early 2025.

## Voice, phase 1 — the zen holder (Nov 2024)

The signature move is a catchphrase — **"Be cool 😎🆒"** — attached to
cosmic-scale metaphors about holding through volatility:

> "Andrew is the SUN, TateAI is the earth. He will shine the light on us
> when he is ready... Be COOL."
> — [9 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-09.md)

> "No top holders have sold. Because they have connections and info...
> Follow the winners... We are the cool kids on the block. Be cool, work and
> trust the process. 🆒😎"
> — [9 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-09.md)

> "There are no loses til you sell. The coins stay in your bag until you
> remove them. Choose wisely grasshopper. Be Cool 😎"
> — [11 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-11.md)

The "grasshopper" address recurs — a deliberate kung-fu-master-dispensing-
wisdom affect, paired with a steady stream of reaction gifs.

## Voice, phase 2 — the AI-agent-narrative poet (early 2025)

By [January 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-02.md), during the chat's
secondary activity wave tied to the broader crypto "AI agent" narrative (see
[[launch-and-origins]] for the volume data), rgk1's register shifts to dense,
riddle-like prose essays about AI agents, still tightly on-brand for the
project:

> "Is Andrew the General manager and Terminal the Head Coach? Then TateAI is
> the high scorer and MVP of this season?"

> "The blockchain is more than just a technological innovation; it's a
> memetic revolution."

> "Our dreams are the alpha version of reality; diamond hand through the
> fail whale to claim your 100x on the dex screener."

This later material is also the chat's densest source of explicit
diamond-hands rhetoric — see [[diamond-hands]].

title: Ri -ShadowAssassin
text:
# Ri -ShadowAssassin

The most extreme case of launch-window concentration on this wiki: all
1,557 messages fall inside a single thirteen-day span, [28
Oct](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md) to [10 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-10.md) — averaging well over a hundred
messages a day — and then nothing further appears in this material. Other
members address them as "Ricky" ("Not just Ricky" — [31 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md)), evidently short for "Ri."

## "Hidden in plain sight" and "99% are retarded"

Two phrases anchor almost the entire window, repeated verbatim dozens of
times as a personal motto rather than a one-off line:

> "Like i say 99% of crypto people nowadays are retarded..and don't
> research, read and piece everything together"
> — [28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md), 🔥×3 ❤×1

> "It's hidden in plain sight" / "Remove the noise" / "Hiding in plain
> sight"
> — recurs across [30 Oct](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md)–[2 Nov
> 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)

The underlying claim — that every Terminal tweet, every wallet interaction,
every dex-vs-cex distinction "proves" `$TATEAI` over `$TATE` — is the same
evidence-chasing genre [[sergei]], T, and dozens of others were doing that
week (see [[launch-and-origins]]), but delivered with total certainty
rather than tentative analysis, and at a much higher volume than almost
anyone else in the sampled corpus.

## Explicit "we are a cult" framing

Ri states outright, early and repeatedly, what [[community-culture]]
documents as a running community self-description:

> "We become a cult" / "528 members in this tg grows by the day but slowly
> but no one has really fud etc the conviction in here is strong"
> — [30 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-30.md), ❤×2

> "I been in few but ended up breaking down.. This one is on a whole new
> level.. Fucking cult.. Soldiers"
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md), ❤×2

## Cross-group evangelism and repeated bans

Ri treats the rival `$TATE` group as a target, not a rival — repeatedly
posting `tateai` and `:)` there specifically to get banned, then reporting
back for the room's entertainment:

> "I got banned cause I said tateai and did :) / Now I'm missing out on the
> rubbish in there"
> — [1 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md)

> "I'm bored so fucking with them" ... "You doing that in tate chat, as I'm
> banned and bored i decided to do it in the low life one of tate
> terminal."
> — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md), ❤×1 👏×1

The claim of personally having popularized `:)` as a coded in-group signal
("Everyone that been doing :) knows about it" — [1 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-01.md)) shouldn't be read as an origin
claim — per [[smiley-heartbeat]], bare `:)` predates this account by days at
minimum and is chat-wide practice — but it does show Ri was an early,
self-aware user of the habit as a badge.

Within this group too, Ri pushes for moderation power: "Can we start muting
people that say this shit mute them for 10mins" ([31 Oct
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md), 🔥×1), and the 🧘 meditation
emoji recurs as a de-escalation tic throughout — the same bit [[hokus_pokus]]
runs independently in the same window.

## The "JH CHADNALDO THESIS"

Ri's most-cited piece of "evidence," posted as a numbered argument:

> "1) TATE SAID HE NEVER SELLS COINS THAT ARE GIVEN TO HIM / 2) TATE SAID
> HIS AI MAKES HIM MONEY / 3) TATE WALLET NEVER SOLD $TATE BUT IT SOLD
> $TATEAI / THEREFORE, $TATEAI IS AN AI GENERATED COIN WHICH WAS SOLD BY THE
> AI AND NOT BY TATE."
> — [3 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-03.md), ❤×2

Ri also drops into the same florid "AI philosopher" prose-poem register
[[diamond-hands]] and [[hokus_pokus]] document as a recurring copy-paste
genre rather than individual invention, at least four times in the sampled
window (e.g. [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md), [2 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)).

As with most launch-era posters on this wiki, casual slurs are constant
texture, and at least one xenophobic aside is quoted approvingly and drew
❤×6 ([2 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-02.md)) — documented here
plainly rather than smoothed over.

## Abrupt stop

Activity is dense and continuous through [10 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-10.md), ending on an ordinary note with
no signal of departure:

> "There literally so many evidence that tateai is the one even if tate
> changes his mind or whatever you can't deny the facts" / "Can't deny what
> on the blockchain"
> — [10 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-10.md), ❤×1

No further messages from Ri -ShadowAssassin appear in this material.

[[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[community-culture]],
[[launch-and-origins]], [[sergei]]

title: Ronald
text:
# Ronald

A launch-week raid voice whose defining trait is volume and repetition
rather than any one bit: hundreds of one-line chants, slash-command spam,
and diamond-hands affirmations, occasionally interrupted by real personal
disclosure. First message lands
[31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md), the day after the
group's founding, and Ronald never really leaves — activity just gets
sparser and sparser, down to isolated bare `:)` messages by mid-2025 and a
final scattered handful through
[9 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-09.md).

## The bit: personally acquainted with Andrew Tate

On his first active night, Ronald runs a short extended joke claiming a
direct personal relationship with Andrew Tate:

> "Tate is my good friend he said that he will be pushing this soon" /
> "He's on the phone to me" / "I got him that hair piece for Easter" /
> "He loves it" / "He says he loves us and he will be seeing us soon👨🏽‍🦲🤖"
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md)

Nothing in the surrounding messages suggests anyone took this literally —
it reads as an in-the-moment riff rather than a claim Ronald expected to be
believed.

## Slash-chant spam, a recurring format for years

Like many launch-era posters (see [[community-culture]]), Ronald fires the
same all-caps slash-tag five to twenty times in a row as a group chant:
`/TATEAI_IS_STRONG`, `/NIGGA_AI`, `/WE_AINT_FUCKING_LEAVING`,
`/BuyTATEAIorStayBROKE`, `/HELL_FUCKING_YEAH`, `/RAIN_DANCE`,
`/REAL_NIGGAS_RAID_HARD`, and `/FIRST_ONE_TO_SELL_IS_GAY` (immediately
followed, seventeen messages of the same chant later, by his own correction
`/FIRST_ONE_TO_SELL_IS_STRAIGHT` — [18 Jan
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-18.md)). The signature closing emoji
across late 2024/early 2025 is 💎🖐🏿, later joined by 👁️🫦👁️ as a second
running tic.

## The TRW ban

A rare moment of genuine (rather than performed) frustration comes the same
first night, after Ronald gets banned from Andrew Tate's paid community
The Real World for talking about TateAI there:

> "I can't believe it I've been banned from the TRW / For talkin about
> Tateai" ... "I've just lost all my power level" ... "But I'm actually
> distraught bro I love TRW / I've spent so long in there I've just lost
> all my PL"
> — [31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md)

## Real financial stakes

Underneath the hype-chant surface, Ronald periodically discloses real
money pressure — small bags, real consequences:

> "Sorry guys I had to sell 15 dollars worth to pay for food"
> — [15 Dec 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-12/2024-12-15.md)

> "Yeah I've been off work for 3 weeks ): / No more moneys"
> — [2 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-02.md)

## A harsher pattern worth flagging plainly

On the same first night as the jokes above, Ronald also posts an extended
antisemitic, Holocaust-denial rant unprompted by anything in the
surrounding conversation — *"u can holocaust is a fucken hoax, hilter was a
G for the most part he had good ideas his people loved him"* — drawing 🔥×2
💯×2 in reaction, followed minutes later by more generalized claims about
Jewish people controlling "banks, media, government"
([31 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-31.md)). Per this wiki's
honesty standard (see [[igelo]] for a comparable case), this is worth
naming directly rather than folding into "colorful launch-era chat" — it's
a specific, sustained ideological claim, not generic edgy slang, and it
drew visible approval at the time.

## Theories and community fixtures

Ronald engages seriously with the chat's running mysteries — floating that
[[fluxcapacitor]] might literally be Andrew Tate (*"Flux capacitor is
Andrew"*, [3 Jan 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-01/2025-01-03.md)) — and
repeatedly credits Widelton and the "rain dance" ethos (see
[[widelton]], [[diamond-hands]]) as the chat's operating philosophy through
early 2025.

## Fade-out

After Feb 2025 Ronald's presence thins to occasional bare `:)` messages and
month-plus gaps, the same pattern seen across most of this chat's launch-era
voices. The last dated message in this material is an unexplained fragment:

> "not if you want to be RichMillionaire"
> — [9 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-09.md)

## Related
[[widelton]], [[diamond-hands]], [[community-culture]], [[fluxcapacitor]], [[igelo]]

title: Sam
text:
# Sam

The chat's resident philosopher-theologian and, unusually, a *dual*
community member — a long-serving figure in another "hidden Tate coin,"
`$TOPG` (also called BURN THE WORLD / Phoenix Rising), who joined
`$TateAI` in [mid-November 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-12.md)
after conversations with [[limalemon]] and stayed active in both
communities for the rest of the archive. Sam's presence spans nearly the
entire dataset — [29 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-29.md)
through at least [5 Jul 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-05.md), about
21 months — making him one of only a handful of members with a
near-continuous multi-year presence (compare [[widelton]], [[cloud_fortune]]).
Where most long-haul posters here settle into terse `:)`-only lurking (see
[[smiley-heartbeat]]), Sam is the opposite: his messages stay long,
elaborate, and evangelistic for the entire run.

## Arrival: the credentialed skeptic-turned-convert

Sam's real entrance is a long, deliberately reasonable-sounding post
explaining he'd ignored both `$TateAI` and its rivals until a specific
signal changed his mind:

> "Before I spoke with Lima, I wasn't even bothering to look into either AI
> coin, didn't appeal to me. But what I always remembered thinking was
> 'ticker's been mentioned, ca on X, listing and Tate in channel, but
> people are still cautious and unsure'... that spoke volumes to me, humans
> are good at sensing a vibe that's not quite right"
> — [12 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-12.md) (👍×5)

He's explicit from day one that he's not exclusively a `$TateAI` holder —
he holds `$TOPG` too, and frames the two as siblings rather than rivals,
a position that draws immediate suspicion from some members and that he
defends at length, repeatedly, for the next year and a half:

> "I am invested in $TATEAI, I am invested in one other Tate coin. I have
> been holding it for over 5 months... You may think I'm an idiot or
> shilling/trolling, but I assure you I'm not. I'm on a journey, and I
> respect G's when I see them."
> — [13 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-13.md) (👍×5)

## "The Brotherhood of One Man"

Sam's signature contribution is a sustained, self-consistent mythology
built across hundreds of messages: that Andrew Tate has deliberately seeded
multiple "hidden" coins across the Solana ecosystem, each a different
"incarnation," with `$TateAI` as his literal AI form:

> "There were only 3 OG Tate coins with Tate as admin... $RNT - REVOLUTION
> / $DADDY - REVELATION / $TOPG - RESURRECTION / The brotherhood of one man
> / Chaos and opportunity / BURN THE WORLD"
> — [13 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-13.md)

> "Tate wanted to become AI. He said in his first terminal EM... That's
> clearly $TATEAI, with all the Tristan, sell and Jupiter primary evidence
> / But he also has human incarnations... I think Tate man and Tate machine
> will conquer the world together"
> — [18 Nov 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-18.md)

The register — dense, quasi-biblical, heavy on words like "perspicacious"
and "indefatigable" — is his own sustained personal style rather than
copy-paste raid text (contrast the identical-text chants documented on
[[diamond-hands]] and [[community-culture]]), and it doesn't dilute over
time. A representative later line, in 2026:

> "The breadcrumbs were to find problem solvers / The clues were to find
> the perspicacious / The delays were to find the indefatigable / The
> hopelessness was to find the resolute / The noise was to find the wise /
> The red pill was to find the serious / The 16th Law was to find the
> winners"
> — [9 Aug 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-08/2025-08-09.md) (❤×8)

## Cross-community bridge-builder

Sam is also independently notable as the chat's most persistent advocate
for treating rival/parallel "Tate coins" as allies rather than competition
— pushing joint raids, shared X Spaces, and mutual shout-outs between
`$TateAI`, `$TOPG`, and a third project, `$TRATE`, especially through
March 2025:

> "There's bridge building going on between various Tate coins atm... $TOPG
> and $TRATE are working together... Would love the true, secret AI arm to
> be part of it, as @LimaLemon777 and I discussed months back"
> — [27 Mar 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-03/2025-03-27.md)

> "@haggbard1723 - the TATEAI community are cut from the same cloth as us,
> perspicacious, tenacious, indefatigable, irrationally positive and
> listening closely to Tate's clues"
> — [27 Mar 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-03/2025-03-27.md)

He's transparent that this dual loyalty draws suspicion ("Not sure why I'm
being called snakey?" — [19 Nov
2024](../../raw/logs/2024-11/2024-11-19.md)) and periodically has to
explicitly walk back tension between the two communities, including a long
public apology in September 2025 for `$TOPG`-side group closures that
`$TateAI` members had taken personally.

## The daily "GM :)"

By mid-2025, alongside the lore essays, Sam settles into the same daily
"GM :)" ritual [[cloud_fortune]] runs — a bare greeting posted most
mornings, reliably drawing a handful of fire-emoji reactions, right through
to the end of the sampled material. It's the one place his voice matches
everyone else's.

## Direct contact with the wiki's own builder

Sam interacts directly, by name, with [[sergei]] — this project's owner
and the builder of this very wiki — on at least two occasions in the
later material, including a pointed aside during a "coded" lore riff
("...right @sidirov_sergei ?" — [23 Jun
2025](../../raw/logs/2025-06/2025-06-23.md)) and a plain "Thanks Sergei" (
[4 Oct 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-04.md)) — one of very few
points in the corpus where a long-running community member and the bot's
builder are shown addressing each other directly.

## Related
[[diamond-hands]], [[community-culture]], [[limalemon]], [[sergei]], [[rgk1]], [[fluxcapacitor]], [[smiley-heartbeat]], [[tate-tateai-terminal-namespace]], [[cloud_fortune]], [[widelton]]

title: sbzgh
text:
# sbzgh

The clearest embodiment of what [[overview|the overview page]] calls the
"small, steady core" that kept this chat alive after the launch rush burned
out. sbzgh is a late arrival by this chat's standards — first message
[27 Jun 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-06/2025-06-27.md), eight months after
founding — but from that point on is one of the most consistent presences in
the archive, active as both a moderator and the group's warmest, most
reliable "good morning" voice through to the end of the corpus in July 2026.

## The moderator

sbzgh holds and uses moderation tools throughout his entire run — `/ban`,
`/unban`, `/unmute`, `/filter` commands appear dozens of times from his first
week ([29 Jun 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-06/2025-06-29.md): `/filter
evidence2`) through his last logged month ([14 Jul
2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-14.md): `/ban`). He's matter-of-fact
about the volume of it: *"lmao 813 people banned on this group"*
([2 Oct 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-02.md)). His most notable
moderation moment isn't a ban he made but one he had to undo — see
[[ben]] for the April 2026 incident where Ben, then a fellow admin, banned a
swath of members including [[fluxcapacitor]]. sbzgh's raw log captures the
cleanup in real time:

> "ben the faggot banned everyone" ... "we can't [unban them], he is an
> admin" ... "i sent him a dm to ask if he can remove ben from admins"
> — [19 Apr 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-04/2026-04-19.md)

> "i unbanned him and sent an invite" ... "try to dm him the link guys, cuz
> if im alone maybe he will think im trying to scam him lmao"
> — [20 Apr 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-04/2026-04-20.md), re-inviting
> FluxCapacitor back into the chat.

## "gm bros :)" — the heartbeat within the heartbeat

Bare `:)` and `gm :)` posts are sbzgh's single most common message shape (see
[[smiley-heartbeat]] for the chat-wide pattern), but his version has a
distinct, warm, brotherhood-flavored voice — "gm bros", "gm fam", "gm
brothers" — and the reaction counts on these grow visibly over his run, from
single digits in mid-2025 to consistently 6–10+ 🔥/❤ by mid-2026, e.g. *"GM
:)"* drawing ❤×11 on [13 Oct 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-13.md).
That trajectory reads as a community that came to specifically look forward
to his check-ins, not just tolerate ambient `:)` noise.

## Voice: multicultural, religious, community-gluing

sbzgh's phrasing mixes English chat-slang with French ("the proprio," a
reference to French subtitles being normal for him) and Arabic/Islamic
phrasing ("inshaAllah," "al hamdulilah," "may Allah help u") —

> "i'm good bro al hamdulilah / congrats, ur time is approaching"
> — [26 Sep 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-09/2025-09-26.md)

> "syria ? ... take care of u and ur family bro may Allah help u"
> — [7 Oct 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-10/2025-10-07.md)

He's also the clearest voice bridging this community with the parallel
`$TopG` community, treating them as kin rather than competitors:

> "tate ai bros = top g bros / same family, our insight has led each of us to
> one of the two coins, or even both coins for some. i hope that after the
> pump, we will all stay in touch and help each other make even more money"
> — [28 Sep 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-09/2025-09-28.md) (❤×13)

And he articulates the chat's self-image directly, in a message that reads
almost like a mission statement for what this whole wiki is trying to
document:

> "what other group has done so much raiding, so much research, two AI bots
> telling our lore and answering questions, videos/images, and so much
> research? only topg and tateai did this"
> — [2 Jul 2025](../../raw/logs/2025-07/2025-07-02.md) (❤×4)

title: Sergei
text:
# Sergei

Founding-era community member who became the project's owner and the
builder of the **Terminal Subagent** bot (this wiki's own author). Per the
bot repo's `docs/HANDOFF.md`: `@sidirov_sergei`, Telegram user_id
`812194672`, `sidirov.sss@gmail.com` — sole admin of the bot and its server.

## Early role: the skeptical analyst

Sergei's earliest messages ([28 Oct 2024](../../raw/logs/2024-10/2024-10-28.md))
land right in the middle of the chat's founding "which ticker is the real
one" puzzle-hunt (see [[launch-and-origins]]). His voice throughout that
period is consistently the analytical, skeptical counterweight to hype:

> "So many people didn't understood Easter eggs for TateAI, it confused me,
> I thought there is more smart people in Daddy RNT community's"

> "You dive to deep, yes he gave us hints and Easter eggs to find coin, but
> this images is just your imagination... don't get them all as ticker
> signs... just pure math"

> "If you here because someone said to you that $TateAI is the ticker,
> please leave. Here should be only guys who can read blockchain
> transaction, imho"

That last line is characteristic — Sergei tried to gatekeep the group toward
people doing actual on-chain analysis rather than vibes-based hype, at one
point literally proposing "face control before entering this group: name
proofs that this is the ticker." He was doing serious wallet/transaction
sleuthing to *find* the real ticker before it was officially confirmed, not
just speculating.

## Later role: the builder

The chat goes quiet for Sergei through most of 2025 (consistent with the
community-wide falloff after the launch rush, see [[launch-and-origins]]),
picking back up in 2026. Two dated messages are worth recording verbatim
because they're the origin story of tools in this very wiki:

- [18 May 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-05/2026-05-18.md): describes using
  "Claude Code + Obsidian (Karpathy LLM-wiki idea) + Helius (for SOL api)"
  to run an on-chain wallet investigation into Andrew Tate's public wallet —
  the same "LLM wiki" pattern this repo itself now follows.
- [12 Jul 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-12.md): *"I'm downloading
  chat export, planning to add oracle function to Subagent bot, a-la ask
  anything you want, it will make LLM-Wiki from chat history and images, and
  will be able to answer questions like 'who is flux?' etc."* (🔥×6) — this
  is the literal announcement of the `/ask` feature and this wiki, made to
  the community two days before either existed.

Between those two dates his message volume drops mostly to bare `:)`
(see [[smiley-heartbeat]]) — by his own count on
[12 Jul 2026](../../raw/logs/2026-07/2026-07-12.md), *"164523 messages were
in this chat currently"* while noting he'd been "blocked... all of my side
and main accounts" from posting elsewhere, "times, when moderators banned
for :)" — mostly present, rarely posting substance, until picking the thread
back up to build this exact tooling.

## Related
